places.name: Feel Myanmar
places.description: Tony met with culinary writer Ma Thanegi. They had pig’s-head salad with Kaffir lime leaf, long-bean salad with sesame and fish sauce, pennywort-leaf salad, a salad of Indian-style samosa.

places.name: Min Lan Seafood (Parami Branch)
places.description: Tony had prawn curry here and cringed at the local cultural norm of making kissing noises to call a waiter over.

places.name: Sofaer & Co
places.description: "Once one of the swankest department stores in Rangoon," says Tony. "Now people live here."

places.name: Seit Tine Kya
places.description: Tea shop where Tony had morning tea with U Thiha Saw, a journalist. They had the hot black tea with a dollop of sweetened condensed milk and a fish soup.

places.name: Taung Htate Pan Street
places.description: Tony had a meal with Philippe Lajaunie, restaurateur, former owner of Brasserie Les Halles. They had chicken necks and cold beer.

places.name: Yangon
places.description: Tony visited this town during Thadingyut, the Full Moon festival celebrated by Buddhists at the end of October. He ate street food and watched the human-powered ferris wheels

places.name: Morning Star Tea House
places.description: Not sure if this location still exists, the CNN site says it is on Saya San Road, but not showing up on the map. Tony met with San Zarni Bo and had fermented tea leaf salad, which is served with peanuts, lime, and chilies.

places.name: Old Bagan
places.description: Tony and Philippe traveled on a train through the Burmese country to Bagan. They bought snacks from locals at the train station. The train ride was very bumpy.

places.name: Sarabha Restaurant
places.description: Tony ate at a small food stand near the Bagan Palace ruins and called it the best restaurant in the country so far. He had chicken curry and raved about all the small accompaniments served.

places.name: Bagan Palace Ruins
places.description: Tony and Philippe visited the palace ruins and admired the structures

places.name: 19th Street Night Market
places.description: Tony met with a local punk band at this night market.

places.name: Kaung Myat Restaurant
places.description: Tony and the punk band ate barbecue at this stand in the night market.

places.name: Koreatown Plaza
places.description: Tony and Roy Choi viewed Koreatown from the roof of this building, and talked about the 1992 Rodney King riots that took place below.

places.name: Dong Il Jang Restaurant
places.description: Tony ate with Roy Choi and Roy Kim, the grandson of the founder of the restaurant. They had banchan, bulgogi, sliced ribeye, kimchi bokumbap (fried rice)

places.name: Kogi Taco Truck
places.description: Roy Choi's Korean taco truck. Tony had the Kogi short rib taco, a Korean barbecue short rib on corn tortilla with salsa roja, cilantro, and onion, slaw, and a chili soy vinaigrette.

places.name: Chego
places.description: At the time of filming, this was a food truck, but has since been made into a brick & mortar establishment. Roy Choi took Tony there and he ate a Kimchi Spam Bowl.

places.name: A-Frame (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Roy Choi's A-Frame restaurant.

places.name: Sizzler
places.description: After meeting at David Choe's secret studio where David painted Tony's portrait, the two went to Sizzler to eat. David introduced Tony to his creation, the Salad bar meatball taco.

places.name: Swadesh Restaurant and Grocery Store
places.description: Tony had curried goat, samosas, tandoori chicken, fish curry with chilis in this area called Little Bangladesh

places.name: Jollibee
places.description: Tony and Roy had an Aloha burger, Spam Little Big Bite sandwich, and Halo-Halo, which Tony described as an “oddly beautiful” dessert with  sweet red beans, white beans, red and green jello, young white coconut, flan, ice cream, and shaved ice.

places.name: David Choe's parents' house
places.description: David Choe bought his parents a house in Los Feliz. Tony and David visited the home and Jane Choe, David's mom, cooked for them. She made beef ribs stew, kimchi, chestnut rice, stuffed peppers, seaweed and jellied mung bean, japchae with shiitake, avocado eggrolls, fried squid, potato pancake.

places.name: Myung In Dumplings
places.description: Tony and David Choe had Wang mandu king dumplings (with pork and kimchi inside), mandu dumplings (smaller with thinner dumping skins), and sweet dumplings.

places.name: Monte Carlo Bar
places.description: Tony and David Choe went to this dive bar that Tony loved.

places.name: Beverly Soon Tofu Restaurant
places.description: Tony and Roy Choi visited this place that Roy said was one of his favorites. They serve a soup called Soon Dubu, described by Tony as "a fiery, tongue-searing, ass-burning tofu soup that will make you forget every bad thing you ever thought about tofu."

places.name: La Vanguardia Airport
places.description: "On first inspection, this is an airplane boneyard, where unwanted props from Romancing the Stone corrode artfully. But in reality, this sleepy hangar is an important gateway to the more impenetrable parts of the country," Tony says.

places.name: Miraflores
places.description: The plane landed here in Miraflores, a jungle outpost in the southern province of Guaviare in the Amazon Forest Preserve. Tony talked with a guide there about the cocaine trade of the past.

places.name: Paloquemao
places.description: A large market, where Tony met with a guide to have some fresh carrot and orange juice. They also ate corn arepas. In the back corner of the fish market, they had Caldo de Costilla, a traditional breakfast soup from the Andean region, which has beef short ribs in a broth with potatoes and scallions.

places.name: La Puerta Falsa
places.description: Tony met with Hector Abad, a writer. They ate tamales with chicken and pork belly.

places.name: Tábula
places.description: Tony met with chef and musician Tomas Rueda, and they had crab salad, handmade pasta, stuffed with labneh cheese with a chorizo sauce; braised osso buco (with beef shank) over vegetables, wine and broth in a wood-fired oven.

places.name: Social Club Los Amigos
places.description: Tony met with a band called On the Tropica to play tejo, a game involving "alcohol and explosives." They also ate a Colombian picada, "a huge selection of fried pork, pork rib, steak, cassava, potatoes, and deep fried plantain."

places.name: Sevicheria Guapi
places.description: The Cali version of ceviche: cooked shrimp, slathered in mayonnaise, ketchup, and Worcestershire sauce; rock mollusk with rice; steamed shrimp with green tomatoes; beer; sugar cane (viche), plantains.

places.name: Goat vendor
places.description: Tony met Chef Juan Pablo Mayorga in this town in La Guajira. They ate Frichi, a traditional dish from the Wayuu people, includes the offal of the goat. They then rode ATVs.

places.name: Mayapo beaches
places.description: Tony rested on the beach after crashing on his ATV, had beer, and ate at a restaurant called Mar Azul (which I could not find on the map). They had cazuela, which is a fish chowder made with lobster, fish, shrimp, clams, and conch.

places.name: Ice Fishing on the St. Lawrence
places.description: Tony visited in the winter time and met up with chefs Fred Morin and David McMillan of Joe Beef. They went ice fishing on the St. Lawrence River, then ate a fancy meal in the small cottage on the ice.

places.name: Chef Martin Picard's home
places.description: Tony ate beaver at Chef Martin Picard's home.

places.name: Vinet Park
places.description: Tony watched a game of "shinny hockey," a pickup game of street hockey played on the ice. Meanwhile they ate Choucroute garnie a l'alsacienne (lots of meats and sausages piled on a plate).

places.name: Gare Centrale Train Station
places.description: Tony, along with chefs Fred Morin and David McMillian, traveled by train to Quebec City. The chefs brought along a gourmet meal including foie gras and truffles.

places.name: Le Continental
places.description: Old style restaurant. Lots of tableside service including caesar salad, beef tartare, and shrimp cocktail. David McMillian had the filet mignon, Fred had the Scampi Newberg, Tony had the Dover Sole

places.name: L'Affaire est Ketchup
places.description: The restaurant name means "Everything's Cool" in local slang. They had razor clams, a cream of haddock roe, Coqui St. Jacques, terrine of foie gras, head cheese with cassis mustard, truffled sweetbreads, goose hearts persillade, salt cod.

places.name: Bistro M Sur Masson (Closed)
places.description: Tony met with journalist Patrick Lagacé at this restaurant (closed in 2016). They discussed "pastagate" which is the scandal that happened when a restaurant got in trouble for using the word "pasta" which is Italian

places.name: Wilensky's Light Lunch Inc
places.description: Open since 1932, Tony had the "special,” a beef bologna and salami sandwich with mustard heated on a sandwich press, an egg cream.

places.name: Cabane à Sucre Au Pied De Cochon
places.description: Tony had a meal here that started with a tower of maple desserts. It had a lot of courses which would be too many to list!

places.name: Liverpool House
places.description: Sister restaurant to Joe Beef. Tony met with the chefs and ate Pakistani food by Chef Omar. "Butter chicken crab; little eggplants braised with anar seeds, pomegranate; mushrooms; rabbit korma; fingerlings with fenugreek and fennel; donkey nahari; goat biryani; and more.

places.name: Canadiens de Montréal
places.description: Tony went with the chefs to watch the Montreal Canadiens play the Carolina Hurricanes at the stadium

places.name: Café Tinjis
places.description: Tony had espresso with Jonathan Dawson, British journalist

places.name: Saveur de Poisson
places.description: Restaurant serving tagine. Tony had fresh olives; roasted walnuts; warm bread; tagine with baby shark, calamari, and monkfish with fresh mountain spinach; shark kebabs; strawberries, pine nuts, and honey

places.name: Café Baba
places.description: A cafe serving hashish. "There is one particular cafe in the heart of the kasbah that's drawn in foreign dignitaries, rock stars, aristocrats, and artists since it opened its doors in 1943," Tony explained. Afterward they went directly across the street to a vendor selling spanish omelets filled with potatoes, eggs, ketchup, and mayonnaise.

places.name: Grand Socco
places.description: Tony met with photographer Cherie Nutting and went to this outdoor food market. Tony bought fresh goat cheese wrapped in palm leaves and some flatbread for breakfast.

places.name: Boutique Majid
places.description: Antique shop Tony visited and bought a mortar & pestle style tool from the Gond tribe in Mali for $300.

places.name: Restaurant Andalus
places.description: Tony ate lunch at this locals only place, with Majid, the owner of Boutique Majid. Tomatoes brushed with local olive oil, garlic, and coriander; beef liver kebabs grilled over charcoal; swordfish; orange roughy.

places.name: Tangier
places.description: Tony visited Christopher Gibbs' house (antiques dealer and expat) for a garden dinner party with other expats.

places.name: Bocadillo Abdelmalik
places.description: Tony had a tuna bocadillo here: Spanish style sandwiches with tuna, veggies, and hard-cooked eggs, a healthy wad of mayo, and a crispy layer of french fries in the sandwich.

places.name: Martyrs' Square
places.description: Tony watched as kids skating, fireworks being set off, motorcycles drove around as people celebrated the Prophet Mohammed's birthday and freedom after 50 years of oppression.

places.name: مطعم بركودا 1‎
places.city: Tajura' wa an Nawahi al Arba
places.description: Barakoda Restaurant, a seafood restaurant just outside of town on the Mediterranean. The menu is not printed, you select fish from the daily catch on ice and they cook it for you the way you specify. Tony ate fish, shrimp, and stuffed calamari

places.name: Tripoli
places.description: Tony went to a men-only coffeehouse called Yahzakem Coffee where people smoked hookah and drank local coffee.

places.name: Tripoli Libia
places.description: Somewhere in Tripoli, Tony ate at a place called Uncle Kentucky (or Kentaki) Fried Chicken. Could not find it on any maps.

places.name: Bab al-Azizia
places.description: Former site of Gadhafi's palace, destroyed by rebels in 2011. They were told to leave while they were filming this scene.

places.name: Fashloum
places.description: Somewhere in this neighborhood, Tony had a breakfast of sfinz, a donut with an egg on top.

places.name: Radisson Blu Al Mahary Hotel
places.description: The hotel where Tony and the crew stayed while in Tripoli.

places.name: Misrata
places.description: Tony and crew headed to this town to film, but it was on full lockdown as they approached. They walked around the ruins of the town which showed the damage done when the rebellion took down Ghadafi. Afterward, they killed a lamb and barbecued it on the coast of the Mediterranean

places.name: Leptis Magna
places.description: They stopped to get a liver sandwich at a stand on the way to this spot. Leptis Magna, "arguably the most intact remains of a Roman city in the world."

places.name: Pucusana
places.description: Coque Ossio, one of the most successful chefs in Peru, spends weekends in Pucusana, a small fishing village. His mom, Marisa Guiulfo, is like Peru's Julia Child. They made food and pisco sours for Tony and Eric Ripert. Marisa opens the house every weekend for a large group to visit and eat.

places.name: Chez Wong
places.description: Tony and Eric visited this restaurant where the chef uses a wok to cook. They had octopus and flounder ceviche; tiradito of flounder with pecan, lime, ají limo chili peppers, and sesame oil.

places.name: ámaZ Restaurante
places.description: Tony and Eric visited this restaurant run by Chef Pedro Escofino, highlighting Peruvian ingredients. They started with a lime cocktail with culantro; breadfruit with tuna and cashew sauce; scallops with wild almond and freshwater shrimp dashi; Inchcape soup made of ham, peanuts, and corn; patarashca (catfish wrapped in leaves and cooked on hot coals); paiche (a type of fish from the Amazon River) atop a palm fruit purée with a fermented yucca sauce and a chili pepper sauce made with nuts and ants. Eric did not like the ants.

places.name: Larco Museum
places.description: Tony and Eric visited this museum of pre-Columbian Peruvian artifacts. While there, they went to the erotic gallery and looked at erotic pottery.

places.name: Anticuchos Doña Pochita
places.description: Tony and Eric stopped at this  food stand specializing in beef heart, marinated with garlic, cumin, onion, and vinegar and grilled.

places.name: Chiclayo
places.description: The staging area for Tony and Eric's visit to the mountains to find cacao for their company making chocolate bars.

places.name: Mercado Modelo De Chiclayo
places.description: Eric took Tony shopping in this market to find supplies for a shaman they will visit later in the episode.

places.name: Marañón Province
places.description: Tony and Eric stopped at a roadside stand in the Marañón canyon to have sopa de gallina, a Peruvian hen soup. After they went to the cacao plantation, they had dinner at Don Fortunato's home: juane (rice dumplings with boiled egg, chicken, and achiote) and roasted cuy (guinea pig) in a cacao sauce

places.name: Mercado Municipal Huacrachuco
places.description: Unsure if this is the correct location. There was a scene on the show where Tony and Eric visited Mercado Central on Calle San Martin in the Marañón Canyon where they bought food to cook to feed their host, Don Elioberto, and his family. They made chicken and shrimp with peppers and onions, along with mashed potatoes and traditional Aztec hot chocolate. I could not find a Mercado Central on a street with this name on the map.

places.name: Fatimata Restaurant
places.description: This restaurant is supposed to be in Goma, Rwanda, called Village Fatimata. It specializes in Congolese cuisine like grilled piri piri chicken and gugali. This is the only restaurant with the same name, cannot find it in Goma.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony visited the Wagenia tribe which live in this region. He had steamed tigerfish limboke: steamed in a banana leaf.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony visited this small village town at the base of the Rif mountains and had dinner family style with Bachir Attar, leader of The Master Musicians of Jajouka and his family. Braywine (handformed dough filled with seasoned beef, baked until golden, then crisped in oil); chicken tagine; a wild mountain spinach dish with garlic, cilantro, hot and black peppers, lemon, and olive oil; dessert of fruit and mint tea.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had dinner of salmon marinated in pomegranate juice at a home in this town with Amichai Luria and Amiad Cohen

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Zach's wife's family invited Tony for dinner. Iberico ham, remojon granadino (eaten during holy week, it has salt cod, egg, black olives, oranges, and tomatoes dressed in olive oil), migas (sardines, chorizo, melons, and peppers)

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had dinner at Tomaso's mother's home. They had caponata; bread and tomato salad; shrimp sautéed in garlic, butter, and herbs; sardines fillet sautéed in garlic, oil, and red pepper

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate iftar (meal at sundown when fasting for Ramadan has broken) with the Rasdien family. Malaysian family, they prepare panang curry with beef, a chicken pie, traditional ramadan shake.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony met with Alexander Lebedev, businessman and owner of opposition newspaper Novaya Gazeta, at his home. They had fresh potato chips and Scottish salmon with cherry-tree sawdust.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate at Boi Tran’s home and gallery with Boi Tran, painter; Nguyen Qui Duc; Hương Lan, writer and food blogger; art collector Jean-François Hubert, and Philippe Damas. They had bird’s nest soup, made of swallow’s nests and served with crabmeat, crab roe, red onion, pepper, and seasoned steamed lotus seeds; lobster with red onion, ginger, lemongrass, and chiles, cooked and presented in a bowl with lime leaves and the stock poured over it

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had more Persian food in someone's home. They ate Boz Ghormeh (slow cooked lamb in yogurt, saffron, and egg yolks), sour cherry rice, koofteh tabrizi (a very large meatball with ground beef, onion, cooked rice, walnuts, dried apricots, boiled eggs, and barberries).

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate at local's home, she is a journalist for Havana Times. They had pork marinated in garlic, onion, and sour orange, served with rice.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.city: Southern Nations Nationalities and People's Region
places.description: Tony, Marcus, and Maya went to this small village where Maya grew up. They were served coffee. Later they had a feast where they barbecued 2 goats.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Chef VJ Dennis cooked some gullah classics here at Mosquito Beach on James Island, home of Ashley Green. They had local blue crab, garlic shrimp, softshell crabs & conch in a peanut stew with sauteed Carolina squash and zucchini

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate with Fig owner and executive chef Mike Lata, Rebellion Farm owner Jeff Allen, and Sean Brock. They had barbecued turkey, pickled pigs’ feet and collard greens, barbecue coleslaw, potato salad with ramps, baked red peas, mac and cheese

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony met with the local band Keystone. They made him the national dish, adobo. They had adobo with pork and chicken.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate with a family on a local farm, including the Aegan governor. They had stuffed peppers, slow-cooked rooster, rabbit in a wine sauce, and potatoes.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal prepared by Meriko Gubeladze, a star chef at Tbilisi’s Black Lion Restaurant. They had spinach with walnuts and wild capers; pickled carrots; cheese curds with roasted hazelnuts and mint; roasted eggplant seasoned with coriander; kharcho, a stewed chicken cooked slow with ajika chili sauce, onions, marigold flower powder, and blue fenugreek, and cooked & served in a pumpkin.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Not sure if this is the correct location. Tony said the beach was called "Coeur. de Cassazione" but I could not find this on the map. He ate with Pierre Thiam, a renowned chef and cookbook author. They had yassa, made with a local fish called ciof, "cooked simply on the bone, wrapped in foil, and steamed over the fire, served with a mignonette style sauce with onions; grilled mussels, sea urchin."

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal with locals. They ate mulet farci, a stuffed baked local fish called mullet, stuffed with bread and vegetables and baked.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate the Senegal national dish with some locals. The national dish is called ceebu jen (meaning rice with fish). The fish they used was "Senegalese grouper, scored and stuffed with a mixture of garlic, parsley, and peppers, then slow simmered in a hearty tomato broth, infused by a funkatizing goodness brought by fermented conch and salt fish, served over rice and vegetables."

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony met with Nico (lead singer of band Octafonic) to have a meal at a construction site. In Argentina, construction workers often have barbecued beef together at work once a week.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Chef friends put together a meal with local seasonal ingredients: "90-day aged ribeye steaks from nearby Bear Creek Farms... topped off with oyster mushrooms and bourbon soy, and some nice runny eggs, and garlic. Grits, greasy beans, homemade ricotta with a cold smoked sausage, and roasted acorn squash.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony visited singer-songwriter Margo Price's home where her musician friends had a party and prepared food. Her husband and bass player in the band, Jeremy, made biscuits topped with pimento cheese and bacon; braised short rib on crostini with goat cheese.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony went to Principal Jonathan Nguyen Trinh's home to have lunch. They had tamales de gallina; fresh pupusas on the grill; a jellyfish, shrimp, and pork dish tossed with mint, peanuts, carrots, red onion, and dressed with fish sauce; mi quang: a Vietnamese noodle dish made with pork ribs, shrimp, and chilies; and “Viet-bayou style” crawdads steamed with sriracha, orange juice, and beer

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal cooked by Zora Santos, "a practitioner of old school Afro-Mineira cuisine." They had angu (cornmeal and milk cooked for hours), ora pro nobis (a native green, filled with protein), pork ribs.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Meal on a family farm with a collection of chefs. They had "smoked pig's head with vegetables and chilies, native green beans called andu prepared with bacon and farofa" (toasted cassava flour). Also, "chicken in brown sauce baked in a clay pot, finished with the blood of chickens fresh killed this morning, served with pequi rice, farofa, and of course, cachaca."

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony visited this small village in northern Laos. The villagers cooked him chicken and veggies.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal with Chef James Syhabuot and his friends/family. They had aw lahm, which is made with wood ear mushrooms, dill, pepper wood (a key ingredient infused into the dish, which imparts flavors of star anise and white pepper), two types of lahm made with buffalo (one raw, one cooked), and leuat paeng, a dish made from raw buffalo blood and herbs.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a duck curry made by his host.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Bayshore is one of the priciest neighborhoods in the country. "I'm here as a guest of the Sabgavuds, a successful family of Lebanese Syrians," says Tony.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony witnessed the town get together to slaughter a pig and cook it for a feast.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate with Dr. Ravi Perumalpillai, his sister Jeeva Perumalpillai, and Punitham “aunty” Selvadura. The aunty made crab curry.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony ate at Chef Erica Connelly's home with her family. She made lobster, coconut arepas, and conch salad.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had sauteed chicken gizzards with green bananas, codfish fritters, carne guisada, and mofongo with the band Fiel a la Vega.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had dinner with the McKinney family, “a typical expression of hard-scrabble Appalachian practicality,” combined with “Neapolitan roots”: spaghetti pizza, chicken stew with peas and onions, pumpkin pie cake.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony visited Mariam Movsisyan’s grandmother’s family home. They had ghapama (traditional dish consisting of a baked pumpkin or squash stuffed with rice, nuts, raisins, and apricots), Armenian cheese and lavash, hummus.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal of khash (stock flavored by roasted-beef bones) with lavash.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal at Anton Newcombe's studio (frontman of the Brian Jonestown Massacre) and friends. The had roasted lamb, sweet potatoes, and homemade schnapps.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had lunch with Toby Rodriguez and Marc Savoy, a musician and Cajun-style accordion builder, and his family. They had whiskey, gumbo, and beer.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Toby Rodriguez hosted a crawfish boil here. They also ate jambalaya.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had beef, fried chiles, and biscuits with the Means family on their ranch.

places.name: Meal with locals
places.description: Tony had a meal at Rainer Judd's house (President of the Judd Foundation). They had blue corn tortillas, menudo, sopapillas, queso fresco with chiles, tamales

places.name: Kisangani
places.description: Tony ate at a BBQ kiosk in the city where he had goat stew.

places.name: Kisangani Railway Station
places.description: Tony visited this rail station that the Belgians built during their occupation of the Congo. About 200 volunteers still work at the station to keep it running. Tony took a ride on the train

places.name: Congo River
places.description: Tony had a dream to travel on the Congo River. He cooked some coq au vin on the boat. Later he and crew were stuck here due to technical issues with transportation

places.name: Yangambi
places.description: National Institute for Agronomic Study of the Belgian Congo is located here and Tony met with a man who works to save the library of thousands of books here.

places.name: Western Wailling Wall
places.description: Tony is not a religious person, half Jewish and half Catholic, he felt uncomfortable here at the Wall.

places.name: Damascus Gate
places.description: Writer Yotam Ottolenghi took Tony to East Jerusalem, where Palestinians live. They had falafel from a street vendor

places.name: West Bank
places.description: Tony visited the Palestinian side, referred to as the West Bank or the Samaria, Palestine, or Judea.

places.name: Eli
places.description: Tony and guide stopped to view this settlement and talked about the conflicts between Palestinians and Jews

places.name: Ramallah
places.description: On the edge of this town, Tony visited an all-women racing team

places.name: Alrowwad Cultural and Arts Societyجمعية الرواد للثقافة والفنون‎
places.description: Palestinian children from the Aida Refugee Camp participate in a theater production here

places.name: Bethlehem
places.description: Tony visited Aida Refugee camp and had a meal at the martyr center.

places.name: Majda
places.description: "It feels like an alternate universe..." Tony says of this restaurant run by a couple, where the husband is Muslim and the wife is Jewish. He ate fried eggs with peppers from the garden; roasted tomatoes, okra, onions, and mint; grilled zucchini covered in fried yogurt sauce; fresh zucchini with mint and tiny sweet apricots

places.name: Gaza
places.description: Tony had a meal with Laila El-Haddad and her family. They ate maqluba (a traditional Palestinian dish made of layers of fried eggplant, tomato, potatoes, caramelized onions, and chicken, sautéed then simmered in a broth with nutmeg, cinnamon, cardamom, and rice). They traveled just outside Gaza City to a gathering place for elders where they had fatit ’ajir (roasted baby watermelon, eggplant, tomatoes, squash, chiles, and olive oil) served with unleavened bread.

places.name: מידס‎
places.description: Tony spoke with the owner of this restaurant whose daughter was killed. They discussed both sides of the conflict.

places.name: Bar Aliatar Los Caracoles
places.description: Tony and his Director of Photography Zach Zamboni (who lives part time in Andalucia with his wife), ate a lunch of snails in almond sauce at this eatery with seating outdoors.

places.name: Hacienda La Marquesa
places.description: Tony visited La Marquesas Ranch, the private bullring of star matador David Fandila. Tony tried it himself with a calf. They then ate bull stew with local herbs, onions, and potatoes; ham; homemade chorizo; Spanish cheese; bread with olive oil

places.name: El Tabernaculo
places.description: Tony visited during the Holy Week and stopped here to have some sausage and drinks

places.name: Museo de La Zambra - Cueva de Curro
places.description: In Sacromonte, a neighborhood made of cave structures. Tony watched a an jeurga "informal, intimate, and spontaneous performance"

places.name: Taberna La Tana
places.description: A little place for tapas. Tony went with Zach and Fuen. They had salmon with tomato, caviar

places.name: Restaurante Oliver
places.description: Bar #2 for Tony, Zach, and Fuen. They had mussels steamed in butter and olive oil.

places.name: Bar Gallardo
places.description: Bar #3 for the group. They ate cheese, fried smelts, picarones (fried pastry), chulatitas (baby lamb chops).

places.name: Beach barbecue
places.description: Tony went to a moraga (a beach barbecue)  with chefs Juan Andres Moria and Rafael Luca, and friends. They had dried tuna on toast with spicy olive oil, fish, cheese, pate, grilled octopus, sea bream, pork tenderloin, and morcilla (blood sausage).

places.name: Casa Pasteles Albayzin
places.description: Cafe con leche and a small breakfast like toast with tomato.

places.name: El Taco Loco
places.description: Taco truck where Tony ate carne asada, al pastor, and lengua tacos

places.name: Ghost Ranch
places.description: Tony went horseback riding in this area near where Georgia O'Keeffe spent her last years. Bourdain helped with a red canyon cook-out.

places.name: Santa Fe Plaza Inc
places.description: A mish mash of trinkets and souvenirs for sale, including Native American jewelry.

places.name: Five & Dime General Store
places.description: Tony bought a Frito Pie and a soda

places.name: Horseman's Haven Cafe
places.description: This place has a strain of chile grown specifically for the restaurant. Tony ate enchiladas with carne adobada (pork marinated in red chile sauce), beans and rice, pozole (hominy), sopapilla (fried bread) and green chile salsa (level 3 of spiciness, which Tony said was painful).

places.name: Kirtland Air Force Base
places.description: Two members of the New Mexico UFO & paranormal forum, Norio and James, met Tony outside here to talk about America's first rocketships.

places.name: Dead Horse Ranch
places.description: Margaritas and beer, and a whole roast pig is cooked underground in a barbecue known as a matanza.

places.name: Noma
places.description: Tony visited Chef Rene Redzepi at this restaurant, which is known to many as "the best restaurant in the world." Tony came to Denmark solely to visit this restaurant.

places.name: Tivoli Gardens
places.description: The second oldest amusement park in the world. Tony and Rene visited and rode on the ferris wheel.

places.name: Funchs Vinstue
places.description: Tony tried a local drink called Gammel Dansk (Danish bitters)

places.name: John's Hotdog Deli
places.description: Tony had the deluxe: sausage made with bacon and garlic, homemade mustard, onions pickled in beer.

places.name: Monaci delle Terre Nere
places.description: Agri tourismo - a working farm, hotel, restaurant. Produces olive oil. On his last day in Sicily, he had a meal here made from ingredients on the farm: penne with sundried tomatoes and zucchini; a rabbit dish; agridolce greens

places.name: Spiaggia libera di Isola Bella
places.description: The beach where they launched the boat to go "fishing" and then dropped dead fish, octopus, and shellfish into the water around Tony. He described his feelings on this experience saying he slid "quickly into a spiral of near hysterical depression." After this scene he gets really drunk.

places.name: Osteria Nero d'Avola
places.description: Tony had dinner here after the disastrous "fishing" expedition. It's his birthday and he doesn't remember any of the meal because he drank so much. Tomaso and his wife (locals) had the meal together. They ate white olives, bread, olive oil, abalone served raw in the shell, baby sardines called neonata with citrus and salt, octopus, tuna tartare, cuttlefish crudo, pasta alfino querta (a true Sicilian classic made with fennel, pine nuts, saffron, and anchovy. Served with anchovy curing in chestnuts and salt for days, then lightly fried in oil.

places.name: Trattoria Piccolo Napoli
places.description: Tony met with food writer Mary Taylor Simeti, food writer, for lunch. They had panelli (fritter made from chickpeas), caponata (sweet and sour eggplant), spaghetti al nero di seppia (spaghetti in cuttlefish ink), casarecce al pescespada (pasta with swordfish, eggplant, and tomatoes)

places.name: D'Antone
places.description: Tony tried horse meat here. They grilled it outside on the street. Tony enjoyed it.

places.name: Parco dei Nebrodi
places.description: Tony went to a free range pig farm located in this national park. They cooked one of them, using the lungs and liver in a stew with garlic, chili pepper, red wine. They also make blood sausage and serve it with homemade cured meats, local cheese, and homemade wine.

places.name: Catania Market
places.description: Very old market selling meats, produce, cheese, and other local goods. The market is best known for its seafood.

places.name: Soweto
places.description: Tony watched a soccer game at Mawilies Inn (could not find on the map) in Soweto, a suburb of Johannesburg. The town name derives from an abbreviation of Southwestern Township. They ate lamb's head; mieliepap (sticky porridge made from maize); stewed beef, dumplings, stewed greens, carrots, beans, gravy.

places.name: The Yeoville Dinner Club
places.description: Chef Sanza cooked for Tony: A Nigerian dish of egusi and beef (cooked with melon and pumpkin seeds) futu (crumbly cornmeal porridge); basmati rice with rose water; achar (pickled eggplant and mango); Congolese cassava; and falafel. Menu changes daily.

places.name: Hillbrow
places.description: "A notoriously dangerous district" where Tony met with DJ Les.

places.name: Sympathy's Restaurant
places.description: Tony and DJ Les had fried chicken, greens, mieliepap, beets, and coleslaw.

places.name: Pretoria
places.description: Administrative center of South Africa and once "the heart of apartheid"

places.name: Maders Slaghuis
places.description: A butcher that sells biltong, a salted dried meat. Afrikaner paraphernalia hangs in the shop, which bothered Tony. For restaurant, you can go to the meat counter and order cuts of meat with "monkey gland sauce" on the side (barbecue sauce).

places.name: Neighbourgoods Market
places.description: Tony ate a Balkan burger here. It is a flattened ground beef patty with tons of cheese and your choice of toppings. There is a rooftop with a view where you can sit and eat.

places.name: Robot Restaurant
places.description: Tony said this was the greatest show he's ever seen, better than Jimi Hendrix, Janis Joplin, and David Bowie live. He saw the show called Robot Wars.

places.name: Bar Albatros
places.description: "Definition of a hole in the wall," says Tony.

places.name: Toshima City
places.description: There used to be a boxing gym in Toshima called Yurakura Gym, but I could not find it on the map.

places.name: Yasuda
places.description: Tony loves Yasuda in NYC, and wanted to find out why the chef, whom the restaurant is named for, would suddenly move back to Tokyo. Yasuda said that Japan is the origin of sushi, so he wanted to return to where it was created. Yasuda is also a karate master.

places.name: Daitoryo
places.description: Tony meets with Tamika (a professional dominatrix; Kinoko Hajime, a shibari (Japanese form of rope bondage) artist and Tamika’s partner; and Naga, a translator also joined) at this Izakaya restaurant. They have Kusaia, which is grilled fermented fish, yakitori, and motsunabe (intestine stew with miso).

places.name: Mienoumi
places.description: Tony had a meal with Toshio Maeda, the manga illustrator credited with inventing tentacle porn. They ate katsuo no tataki (seared bonito with daikon, fresh greens, sprouts, and ponzu) and tomato nabe (a hot pot of meat and vegetables favored by sumo wrestlers).

places.name: Mahakala
places.description: Tony had a meal with Chef Yasuda, they had kushiage (fried skewers of shrimp and basil, lotus fruit, octopus, and quail eggs) and okonomiyaki (egg-batter pancake stuffed with squid). Yasuda lives near here and loves this place.

places.name: Packard Automotive Plant
places.description: Tony visited Al Hill, who lives on the site of the 3.5 million sq ft auto plant that is no longer in use.

places.name: Duly's Place Coney Island
places.description: This place has been around for over 90 years. Open 24 hours. The Coney dog has chili, cheese, onions, and mustard atop a hot dog.

places.name: Cutters Bar & Grill
places.description: Tony met with Adolph Mongo, a political strategist, and had a vodka.

places.name: The Heidelberg Project
places.description: Tyree Guyton created this "delightfully loony outdoor community art project that began in 1986 and now attracts 35,000 visitors per year from around the world."

places.name: MT. Elliott Charlevoix Market
places.description: Tony visited this liquor store to pick up some alcohol.

places.name: Greedy Greg's Soul Food
places.description: A backyard BBQ serving soul food. Tony had smoked pork loaded collards and mac & cheese.

places.name: Engine 23 Squad 3
places.description: Tony visited this fire department to eat with the firefighters. They made crabcakes, broccoli salad, and steak.

places.name: 1301 Broadway St
places.description: Former site of Guns & Butter, where Tony ate with Charlie Leduff, a journalist,  at a pop-up restaurant with Chef Craig Lieckfelt.

places.name: Pupuseria Y Restaurante Salvadoreno
places.description: Home cooked Salvadoran meals. Tony ate here with Chef George Azar and friend Joe. They had tamales, pupusas, and shrimp with garlic butter.

places.name: Pritam Dhaba
places.description: "Get used to eating a lot of vegetarian. India is one of the few places on Earth where, even for me, that's not a burden," Tony says. He had chickpeas, saag, and lots of naan.

places.name: Golden Temple
places.description: Tony called this the "Sikh equivalent of the Vatican." He attended a festival called a gurpurab where feet are cleansed and people of all religions all take part in a simple meal

places.name: All India Famous Amritsari Kulcha
places.description: Iconic dish of Punjab. "A perfect little flavor bomb of wheat dough pressed against the side of a very, very hot clay oven, slathered with butter and served with a chickpea curry on the side."

places.name: Beera Chicken House
places.description: Tony had tandoori chicken and fresh baked naan

places.name: Tea Stall And Snacks Stall
places.description: Tony had some snacks at this train station on his way to the Himalayas.

places.name: Shimla
places.description: "Here the colonials created England in miniature," Tony says of this hill town.

places.name: Chapslee
places.description: Tony stayed at this place where a maharaja once lived. He ate some of the food such as mulligatawny soup.

places.name: Bootlegger Italian Bistro
places.description: A locals' joint with live entertainment.

places.name: Raku
places.description: Japanese Izakaya that is fancy and pricy, but casual. Tony met with Penn Jillette and ate sea urchin, tuna, Japanese river crab, hasaki sashimi, hasaki belly, juicy deep-fried chicken, hamachi belly, and fish collar

places.name: Marquee Nightclub & Dayclub
places.description: Tony met with EDM DJ Jason Lima to talk about Vegas nightlife, which he admitted he is too old to understand.

places.name: Oscar's Steakhouse
places.description: Tony met with former mayor and mob lawyer, Oscar Goodman.

places.name: eat.
places.description: Tony had a meal with artist Anthony Bondi at this breakfast and lunch spot.

places.name: Lotus of Siam
places.description: Tony ate here with Chef Jet Tila, who said this place has the "best Thai food in Vegas by far." You MUST have northern Thai food here. Tony says DO NOT order the pad thai. They had nam prik (a composed room temperature salad); nam kao tod (a puffed crispy rice salad with preserved pork sausage); hung lay (pork curry braised with garlic and ginger); and Northern larb.

places.name: é by José Andrés
places.description: Tony ate Iberico ham and bean soup with roasted garlic; nitro almond cup, a frozen shell made from a puree of Marcona almonds, filled with almond milk espuma and finished with Andalusian caviar; mussels, olives with an escabeche “air;” truffle egg, a gelee of truffles formed into an egg shape with an actual yolk suspended in the center; crispy chicken skin escabeche; salt-roasted foie gras and clementine sauce; secreto de cerdo en papillote.

places.name: Huntridge Tavern
places.description: Dive bar where Tony drank some Jameson Black

places.name: Reynon
places.description: Tony ate sabodet (sausage made from pig’s head, tongue, and beef) with Chef Georges Reynon and Chef Daniel Boulud.

places.name: L'Institut Restaurant
places.description: One of the nation's greatest culinary schools, started by Paul Bocuse. Tony and Daniel Boulud tried the poulet en vessie (chicken cooked inside a pig’s bladder).

places.name: Saint-Pierre-de-Chandieu
places.description: Tony visited Chef Boulud's childhood school in this town, where Chef Boulud explained the fresh, healthy meals students are given here. They had pumpkin soup, fish with couscous, cheese with chocolate and orange segments.

places.name: Café Comptoir Abel
places.description: Tony ate here with Chef Boulud and Bill Buford, a former fiction editor at The New Yorker. They had quenelle de brochet en gratin maison (gratin dumplings)

places.name: Bouchon Comptoir Brunet
places.description: Bill Buford called this "a very male tradition," called the Franc Machon. They ate Blanquette de Veau (slowly stewed neck and shoulder of veal with mushrooms served over rice); bread, Beaujolais wine.

places.name: Troisgros
places.description: Chefs Michel and Cesar Troisgros carry on the family tradition of nouvelle cuisine. Tony had salmon in sorrel sauce, a dish that he says changed the world.

places.name: Paul Bocuse Restaurant
places.description: Tony said it had always been his dream to eat here. Paul Bocuse is considered the father of modern French cuisine. They ate soupe aux truffes noires V.G.E. (black-truffle soup named after former French President Valéry Giscard d’Estaing); loup en croûte feuilletée (sea bass in pastry crust); choron (béarnaise or hollandaise sauce made with tomato purée); pot-au-feu (French beef stew); lièvre à la royale (roasted hare).

places.name: Escuela de Box Rudy Perez
places.description: Tony visited this boxing gym to speak with boxer Jorge Lacierva

places.name: MIGAS La Guera
places.description: Located in a large public market in the neighborhood of Tepito, which is known to be a rough area to live in. Tony and hosts had migas: Cracked ham bones stewed with garlic, onion, cascabel chiles, and epazote; thickened with stale bread and served with tortillas.

places.name: Máximo Bistrot
places.description: Tony ate in Chef Eduardo Garcia's kitchen at this restaurant, one of the hottest places in Mexico. He ate abalone with chiles, lemon, and brown butter; confit of suckling pig, "topped with grandma’s salsa and served on a tortilla."

places.name: Monte Albán
places.description: The Zapotec ruins are here. Tony and his guide drank mescal here.

places.name: Central de Abastos Oaxaca
places.description: Chef Alejandro Ruiz Olmedo and Tony visited this Central Market where they ate barbacoa tacos, consommé, and tlayudas (perhaps best described as a Oaxacan pizza).

places.name: Restaurante Tlamanalli
places.description: Tony visited a small town called Teotitlán del Valle and met Abigail Mendoza, a descendant from pre-Spanish Mexicans. She cooked a Chicken mole for him, using 35 kinds of chiles. The dish takes 2 weeks to prepare. She also made cow and pork brains cooked with chiles, tomatoes, and yerba santa.

places.name: Espacio Zapata
places.description: Tony spoke with street artist Yescka about revolutionary art.

places.name: Cantina El Danubio
places.description: Tony had drinks with poet and activist Javier Sicilia.

places.name: Metropol Hotel
places.description: Tony met up with Zamir and ate Astrakhan caviar, salted cucumber with honey, Baltic sprat with beetroot, muksun (white fish with maldon salt), and vodka (of course)

places.name: Yornik (Closed)
places.description: Tony and Zamir had a meal with Boris Nemtsov, one of the main critics of Putin, who served as an advisor under Yeltsin (he has since been assassinated in 2015, and this restaurant is now closed).

places.name: 16 Tonn
places.description: Tony had drinks with Travis Link, manager of punk rock band Louna, and Rouben Kazariyan

places.name: Kazansky railway station
places.description: Tony and Zamir took the luxury Grand Express train from Moscow to St. Petersburg. Aboard the train, they had blini with caviar and cold pickled herring, and vodka.

places.name: The Loft Club (Closed)
places.description: Tony met with Xena Robrek, an openly gay artist and filmmaker, and Darya Tarasova, a local fixer and translator at this club to have drinks.

places.name: Cococo (Closed)
places.description: Former site of restaurant called Cococo. Tony and Zamir had a meal with Sergey Shnurov and Matilda Shnurova, co-owners of Cococo.

places.name: Petropavlovskaya Krepost'
places.description: Former site of a fortress built to protect the city from marauding Swedes, it was taken over by Bolshevik rebels.

places.name: Big Apple Inn
places.description: Tony ate smokes (smoked-sausage sandwich) and ears (pig’s ear sandwiches), both served with housemade hot sauce and coleslaw; hot tamales.

places.name: Soul Wired Cafe
places.description: Tony met with Pyinfamous, a local hip hop artist, to have drinks.

places.name: Quapaw Canoe Company
places.description: Tony went on a canoe tour on the Mississippi River.

places.name: Buck Island
places.description: Tony and the canoe guide stopped here to cook food on a fire.

places.name: Doe's Eat Place
places.description: Opened in 1941. Tony had a Delta dinner of Doe’s famous salad, hot tamales, fries, shrimp, and porterhouse steaks.

places.name: The Senator's Place
places.description: Tony ate classic Southern soul-food buffet of collard greens, fried okra, mac ’n’ cheese, lima beans, red beans, neck bones, rice and gravy, a piece of fried chicken.

places.name: Lusco's
places.description: Tony had the special salad with Italian vinaigrette, steak, shrimp, onion rings, and pompano (a huge Delta fish).

places.name: Rowan Oak
places.description: The home of writer William Faulkner. Tony visited this house with Jack Pendarvis (animated series creator of Adventure Time).

places.name: City Grocery
places.description: Tony went to this writer/journalist bar where they had snacks and drinks

places.name: Lamar Lounge (Closed)
places.description: Former location of Lamar Lounge, a whole hog barbecue place.

places.name: Chiang Mai
places.description: Tony met Andy Ricker (Pok Pok restaurants) in a small village in this region.

places.name: Him Tang Pig's Blood Soup
places.description: Tony and Andy Ricker visited this small but popular restaurant. They had grilled pig tail, Northern Thai herbal pork sausage, pig's brain, and pig's blood soup.

places.name: Laap Kao Cham Chaa
places.description: Tony and Andy ate fried frog with crunchy fried garlic.

places.name: Zoom Bar
places.description: Tony and Andy had grilled pork intestine and drinks.

places.name: Chang Phuak Gate Night Market - Food Stalls
places.description: Known as the Khao Ka Moo Cowboy Hat Lady, where they ate Khao kha moo (stewed pork leg).

places.name: Gia Tod Teing Keun
places.description: Tony and Andy had deep fried chicken and fish, Thai chili dip nam prik noom, Thai pork sausage

places.name: Chiangmai Cabaret show
places.description: Ladyboy cabaret show

places.name: Krapao
places.description: Andy and Tony ate Pad kee mao (drunken noodles) here, a spicy dish that has no noodles for some reason. They also ate kra pao gai (Thai basil chicken), pad cha talay (seafood stirfry).

places.name: Khao Soi Lam Duan
places.description: For breakfast, Tony had Khao soi (curry coconut milk noodle soup), som tam (green papaya salad), Thai iced tea.

places.name: Pa Daeng Jin Tup
places.description: Andy and Tony had their last meal at this place which serves hammered meat. The owner also presented a special dish to them, "a bitter soup with buffalo tendon spiked with bile," which Tony described as addicting.

places.name: Sak Yant Tattoo
places.description: Tony and Andy both got sak yant tattoos (traditional Thai tattoos)

places.name: Bistrô Brazfood Drink Bar (Closed)
places.description: Tony had capirinhas, chorizo, and other snacks at this outdoor restaurant.

places.name: Streetside bar in Pelourinho (Closed)
places.description: Tony met with Claudio Madureiro and Marília Hughes Guerreiro Costa, at a streetside cafe/bar on a big music and party night in the storied neighborhood of Pelo.

places.name: Mercado da Sete Portas
places.description: Tony met with famous Brazilian artist, Belo Borba in this public market. They ate a fish that can kill you if not prepared properly, served in a spicy stew.

places.name: Acarajé da Dinha
places.description: A food stand that always has a line. Tony had acarajé, "a falafel-like wad of crushed black-eyed peas, seasoned with brown brine shrimp and onions, deep-fried until crispy and golden, with chili spice in dendê oil"

places.name: Praia do Rio Vermelho
places.description: Tony relaxed by the beach and had caipirinhas and fried cheese on a stick.

places.name: Fuchun Xiaolong
places.description: Tony had xiao long bao (soup dumplings), fried pork chop with brown sauce with Professor Zhou Lin, Dean at Shanghai Jiao Tong University

places.name: Chun Restaurant
places.description: "Classic, homestyle Shanghai meals," Tony described of this restaurant that is a favorite of Yao Minji, chief feature reporter for SHINE. No menu, no waiting list. Tony had you bao xia (wok-fried shrimp); hong shao rou (braised pork belly); yan du xian (clay pot soup with bean curd stock, pork belly, tofu, and bamboo)

places.name: The House of Roosevelt
places.description: Tony had a meal with Roosevelt China Investments President Tim Tse. They had French oysters with champagne, New Zealand shrimp, Australian Wagyu beef

places.name: Erguang Wonton (Zhaozhou Road)
places.description: Tony's dinner guest, English teacher Bill Wang, says this is the best place to get wontons. Minced pork, bok choy, rice wine in a wonton dough, served with a mixture of soy sauce, vinegar, chili sauce, sesame oil, and peanut butter. "YOU NEED THIS," Tony exclaims.

places.name: Di Shui Dong
places.description: "Hacker turned entrepreneur Thomas Yao" met with Tony at this place, where they ate ziran paigu (cumin-rubbed ribs).

places.name: Constellation Bar
places.description: Tony and Thomas Yao pregamed here before crashing a wedding. They had moscow mules

places.name: Moodies Records
places.description: Tony met with the record store owner to talk about the birth of hip hop in the Bronx.

places.name: 188 Bakery Cuchifritos
places.description: "Old school, Puerto Rican goodness," Tony said of this place which serves cuchifritos (deep-fried pig parts), morcilla (blood sausage), plantains, rice and beans.

places.name: Pelham Bay Park
places.description: Tony had a fish coconut stew in this park.

places.name: Barry's restaurant
places.description: Tony had pig-tail soup and rum drinks.

places.name: Lammy's Restaurant
places.description: Tony had goat curry, stewed oxtail, rice, peas, collards, mac ’n’ cheese.

places.name: Sea Shore Restaurant
places.description: "Cape Cod of the Bronx." Tony had steamed clams, boiled striper, snow crab, beer.

places.name: Liebman’s Deli
places.description: One of the only Jewish delis in the Bronx. Tony had hot pastrami, chopped liver on toast, potato pancakes with applesauce, pickles, Dr. Brown’s Cel-Ray (celery-flavored soda).

places.name: White Castle
places.description: Tony had the famous sliders here

places.name: Bronx Academy of Letters
places.description: Tony visited this school. They talked about chopped cheese.

places.name: Lido Bar
places.description: "Ladies in orange vests cook and serve old school Paraguayan working class food to people from every walk of life," Tony explains. They ate empanadas de carna (beef, onion, and hard-cooked egg).

places.name: Mercado Cuatro
places.description: A food market with food stands, known as the largest and most popular food market. Tony ate a catfish soup, gnocchi and stew, sopa de Paraguayan.

places.name: Paraguay River
places.description: Tony took a boat up the river to see New Bordeaux. While on the boat, they ate grilled dorado fish topped with a mango salsa and catfish

places.name: Iglesia Divino Niño Jesus en Asuncion Paraguay
places.description: Tony visited this old cemetery to see if he could find his great great great great grandfather's grave. He did not find it but historians said it might have been overbuilt. While in Paraguay he learned that his ancestor came into the country as a hat maker but also sold fireworks, which could have been made into weapons.

places.name: Dong Ba Market
places.description: Tony had bún bò Huế (bone broth with lemongrass), fermented shrimp paste with rice noodles, tender beef shank, crabmeat dumplings, pig’s foot; huyết (blood cake)

places.name: Nhà hàng Duyên Anh
places.description: Tony met with his old friend Dinh Hoang Linh and had grilled eel with lemongrass, green chili, and pepper. And lobster

places.name: Quán bánh O Lé
places.description: Tony had lunch here: bánh xèo (rice-flour pancakes), steamed and topped with fried shallots and cassava paste and crumbled fried shrimp; bánh bột lọc (cassava or tapioca with pork and shrimp wrapped in a banana leaf); fried sticky-rice dumplings filled with shrimp

places.name: Jaw's Corner
places.description: Tony had coffee and a pastry at an outdoor cafe here

places.name: Jambiani
places.description: Tony visited this coastal town, which was an important site to raise the support of revolution for equal rights. A family cooked a meal for him. Coconut rice, freshly caught tasi fish, simmer with garlic and lime, topped with umngqusho (a fresh salad of chopped tomato, eggplant, cucumber, potato), and another fish which is a type of mackeral, marinated in lime and garlic and fried. Served with various breads, cassava, and rice.

places.name: Forodhani Gardens
places.description: Street foods galore! Tony had octopus and masala lobster with chili mango, grilled on a skewer. He also had Zanzibar pizza (beef, tomato, onion, cheese, egg, and mayo rolled up in a dough and fried)

places.name: Stone Town
places.description: Somewhere on the outskirts of Stone Town, Tony visited a vendor that specializes in meats on skewers. He had beef on a stick, chicken (prepared with garlic, lime, coriander, ginger, salt, and pepper), tamarind-chili sauce, fries.

places.name: Arusha
places.description: Tony flew to this small town before heading into the Serengheti.

places.name: Ngorongoro Crater
places.description: "An entire ecosystem within an ecosystem" Tony said of this volcano crater. He had breakfast at his hotel overlooking the crater lake.

places.name: andBeyond Ngorongoro Crater Lodge
places.description: "Idyllic natural setting and good plumbing? Pretty much paradise," Tony describes.

places.name: Lake Magadi
places.description: They had lunch by the lake after driving around looking at zebras, hyena, lions, giraffe, and elephants. There was pesto pasta and brownies involved.

places.name: Ndutu Camp
places.description: Tony and wildlife biologist visited this area where tribal people called Maasai people live and share land with the lions

places.name: (Tabbakhi Neshat) طباخی نشاط‎‎
places.description: Tony had sheephead stew and bread at this cafe.

places.name: DiziBar Persian Cuisine
places.description: Tony had a dish called Dizi, named after the vessel it is cooked in

places.name: Tehran
places.description: The best food in Iran is made at people's houses. Tony's host cooked him a huge meal in her house. They had chicken cream soup, Fesenjan (a stew of fried chicken, onion, ground walnuts, pomegranate, dried apricot, tomato paste), Mahia Halva (tilapia), Ghemieh (beans and meat), Tahdig (crispy rice).

places.name: Bethlehem Church
places.description: Tony visited this church in the Isfahan province

places.name: Haaj Mahmoud Shefa'at Beryani
places.description: Tony had Iranian biryani here.

places.name: Abbasi Great Mosque
places.description: The second largest public square in the world

places.name: Milad Tower
places.description: A symbol of national pride, the tallest building. Tony visited the observation deck. During the shoot, a huge sandstorm rolled through the area, causing parts of the building to fly off. This was all captured on film.

places.name: Spiritus Pizza
places.description: John Yingling owns this place and helped to mentor Tony in his young days when he was not yet a chef. Tony used to sleep on top of the walk-in at Espiritus' old location.

places.name: The Lobster Pot
places.description: Tony had Portuguese Kale Soup with linguisa, and crab-stuffed cod.

places.name: A-House
places.description: America's oldest gay bar (built in 1798). Tony had drinks here.

places.name: MacMillan Pier
places.description: Tony rode on the Sea Hunter, a sea scallop fishing vessel

places.name: Old Colony Tap
places.description: Fishermen bar. They also ate Wellfleet oysters.

places.name: Greenfield
places.description: Tony visited this small New England town to talk about heroin and opiate use.

places.name: Shelburne Falls Bowling Alley
places.description: The second oldest bowling alley in the US, where pin bowling is the game.

places.name: Shady Glen
places.description: Old school diner specializing in many pies. Tony had corned beef and cabbage and raspberry cream pie.

places.name: Recover Project
places.description: Tony visited this drug recovery center where people shared their stories.

places.name: Turners Falls Schuetzen Verein
places.description: Tony went to this local's only shooting club where they had a good old fashioned clam bake

places.name: Piggy's Jerk Centre (Closed)
places.description: Tony ate at a jerk stand called Piggy's in this town. Unfortunately it burned down in 2019

places.name: Trident Hotel
places.description: Tony was the only guest in this "expensive, luxurious" hotel. Tony had dinner with the owner of the hotel, one of the richest men in the world, local billionaire Michael Lee Chin.

places.name: Dr. Hoe Rum Bar
places.description: Tony had a traditional fisherman’s breakfast, and a Steel Bottom (one part rum, four parts beer).

places.name: GoldenEye
places.description: Ian Fleming's cottage (British author known for creating the James Bond character). It is now a resort.

places.name: Chris’s Cook Shop Main Street
places.description: Tony had oxtail, curried goat, callaloo, rice and pigeon peas.

places.name: Cynthia’s on Winifred
places.description: At this public beach, this food stand sells ackee, banana, and salt cod.

places.name: Chef Choi Biryong’s U.S. Army barrack-converted studio
places.description: Tony and Chef Biryong make budae jjigae on his TV show called Mukbang.

places.name: Junco Music Town
places.description: Much to Tony's horror, his business friends decided to go to this karaoke bar

places.name: Gwangjang Market
places.description: Tony has ddukboki (spicy rice cakes), japchae (sweet-potato noodles), and soondae (blood sausage)

places.name: Jeongdaepo
places.description: Korean BBQ with a group of Korean businessmen

places.name: PeaK PC Bang Yeoksam
places.description: Tony played a video game in this PC video game cafe

places.name: Islas Canarias Restaurant
places.description: Cuban coffee and food. Tony met with chef Michelle Bernstein and had a medianoche sandwich (roast pork, ham, Swiss cheese, pickles, and mustard)

places.name: Mac's Club Deuce
places.description: Tony met the owner, Mac Klein, who was 100 years old in 2014 (he died in 2016).

places.name: Captain Jim's Seafood Market & Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with documentary film producers Billy Corben and Alfred Spellman to have stone crab claws.

places.name: Yardbird Southern Table and Bar
places.description: Tony had a meal with Questlove here. They had deviled eggs; fried green tomatoes with pork belly; chocolate chip pancakes with bourbon maple syrup, banana compote, and peanut butter; fried chicken with chilled watermelon; cheddar-cheese waffles; and shrimp and grits

places.name: MLK(My Little Kitchen) Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with Miami bass pioneer, Uncle Luke. They had fish and grits.

places.name: B & M Market
places.description: A market in Little Haiti with a 4-seat cafe selling cow foot soup.

places.name: Pepito's Plaza
places.description: Tony and Chef Michelle Bernstein had a HUGE burger here called the Doralzuela (beef patty with ham, egg, six varieties of sauce, crispy potatoes, and cheese)

places.name: Miami River
places.description: Tony visited Captain Bob who lives on a boat on this river.

places.name: Oolite Restaurant (Closed)
places.description: Tony ate health food with his punk hero Iggy Pop

places.name: Mother India Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with former police chief John Carnochan and had lamb curry simmered in spicy tomato gravy and naan

places.name: Rogano
places.description: Tony met with Janey Godley, a comedian, and had Scottish oysters and lobster thermidor

places.name: Tactical Edge
places.description: Tony met with former bouncer defense expert Mark Davies at his close combat knife defense center

places.name: The University Cafe
places.description: Tony had fish and chips (haddock), covered in shredded cheese and curry sauce; deep fried haggis with curry sauce

places.name: Letterewe
places.description: Tony had a home cooked meal made by Fiona Cullinane here with A.A. Gill, a restaurant critic. They had roasted grouse with bacon and bread sauce, and served with watercress, parsnips, and beetroot. The next day they went hunting and shot a deer, then had a meal of venison seared in duck fat and served with a pan sauce of red wine, butter, and currant jelly.

places.name: New York Café
places.description: Over 125 years old, it was the nicest cafe in the world at the time it was built where artists and writers gathered. Nowadays, they have lots of foie gras on the menu. Tony had the pork throttle stew.

places.name: Bufe Falatozo
places.description: "A smoky, chilly working class joint," Tony describes of this restaurant. "A golden pancake, heaped with chicken livers, and covered, nay, drowned, with a rich, deeply satisfying sauce with bone marrow." He also tried the venison stew and the HUGE schnitzel this place is famous for.

places.name: Belvárosi Disznótoros
places.description: Tony tried the blood sausage with braised red cabbage and pickles.

places.name: Gellért Thermal Bath
places.description: A huge and beautiful thermal bathhouse Tony went to with Vilmos Zsigmond, a cinematographer who worked on movies like Close Encounters of the Third Kind. (He died in 2016.)

places.name: Old Kőrössy Fish Restaurant
places.description: Tony went to a restaurant from Vilmos' childhood, where they ate this fish chowder called halászlé made from pike from the river and seasoned with paprika and other spices.

places.name: Vígszínház Street
places.description: Tony saw a gypsy band called Budapest Bar play and then ate with the musicians. They had chicken paprikash, whole perch roasted in bacon, stuffed cabbage filled with goose meat and slow-cooked, goulash.

places.name: Muguet
places.description: Tony met with Darren Aronofsky here to begin their journey

places.name: Look's Andravoahangy
places.description: Tony and Darren Aronofsky visited this restaurant where Tony ate pork and Darren (a vegetarian) had veggies and beans.

places.name: Chez Mariette
places.description: Tony had a famous goose dish, served with broth made from chicken and ginger, rice, and a salad

places.name: Ranomafana National Park
places.description: Tony and Darren got to see golden lemurs. There are only 500 alive in the world. Tony then watched the village people butcher a cow while Darren took photos and relaxed by a stream.

places.name: Sahambavy train station
places.description: Tony and Darren took a very old train to Manakara.

places.name: Manakara
places.description: Tony ate shark, lobster, shrimp, zebra, lots of veggies (Darren just had veggies) on the beach with some locals.

places.name: Hiram's
places.description: Diner

places.name: Barnegat Light
places.description: Tony and his brother drove out to this shore town to relive old memories.

places.name: Kubel's
places.description: Tony and Chris had lunch here and talked about their childhoods on the beach. They had clams in drawn butter, which he says no matter where in the world he ate them, it reminds him of this spot. They also had fried clams and fries.

places.name: Knife and Fork Inn
places.description: Tony ate pretzel-crusted swordfish over lump crab meat with Vicki Gold Levy, a local historian. She had pan seared scallops.

places.name: Dock's Oyster House
places.description: Tony ate with Brian Donohue, a NJ journalist. Tony had oysters and stuffed lobster.

places.name: Borgata
places.description: Tony watched a comedy show featuring comedian couple Rich Vos and Bonnie McFarlane, then ate a meal with them.

places.name: Tony's Baltimore Grill
places.description: Tony had dinner with the comedian couple. He ate spaghetti with meatballs. Tony says he is not a fan of Trump and says he looks ridiculous.

places.name: Tony & Ruth Steaks
places.description: Tony met with Tawanda Jones, founder of a youth empowerment advocacy to talk about her drill team group. He ate eggs, meat, rice and soup for breakfast.

places.name: Donkey's Place
places.description: Tony says the cheesesteaks here are better than Philly, but it's not a typical cheesesteak. It's on a round kaiser poppyseed roll with steak, fried onions, and American cheese

places.name: Lucille's Country Cooking
places.description: Tony had eggs and meat here with Paul Pederson, a musician from the Pine Barrens

places.name: Frank's Deli & Restaurant
places.description: Tony had a Jersey sandwich with sliced ham, salami, pepperoni, provolone, tomato, onions, shredded lettuce, roasted peppers, and oil & vinegar.

places.name: James' Original Salt Water Tfy
places.description: Tony is not a sweets guy, but he and Chris visited this salt water taffy place, which is a nostalgic place for them

places.name: Town
places.description: Tony had pan roasted mahi mahi in this local seafood restaurant

places.name: Ethel's Grill
places.description: Tony went to this small eatery with 2 chefs, Andrew Lay and Mark “Gooch” Noguchi. They ate miso soup, pigs feet soup, tripe, taco rice (taco toppings and an over-easy egg on top of rice, which is an Okinawan specialty).

places.name: Kalaeloa Harbor
places.description: Tony went diving with a fisherman for some octopus off the coast of Moloka'i, which they hunted with the spear.

places.name: Keawanui Fishpond
places.description: A sacred place in the community on Moloka'i, which is known as "The Friendly Place," but has a reputation for being unwelcoming to outsiders on other islands. They had slow roasted pig, grilled kala fish, mullet cooked lavalu style, octopus, poi, and freshwater clams from the mountains.

places.name: Tasty Crust Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with a Hawaiian journalist, Daniel Ikaikaito. They had a plate lunch, a Hawaiian specialty of a protein with white rice and potato-mac salad. They ate theirs with chicken katsu, hamburger steak in “sinister” gravy, seared ahi with nori and sesame seed.

places.name: Rizk Chicken
places.description: "Broasted" chicken shawarma

places.name: Radio Beirut
places.description: Tony met with a rapper named Chino to talk about this radio station that hosts all types of musicians.

places.name: Al Rawda - Shatila - Restaurant
places.description: Tony had fish, a meze of hummus and salads, Laziza nonalcoholic beer.

places.name: Al Falamanki Sodeco
places.description: Tony had tabbouleh, kofta with yogurt sauce and cranberries, stuffed grape leaves, kibbeh.

places.name: MusicHall Starco
places.description: This huge night club has many different kinds of acts. Tony met with the owner.

places.name: Santy Pescador
places.description: Tony had sushi with Hugo Cancio, a Cuban born entrepreneur

places.name: Fábrica de Arte Cubano
places.description: Nightclub, performance space, and art gallery highlighting artists and DJs from around the world.

places.name: Restaurante Tien Tan
places.description: Tony had a meal with Jon Lee Anderson, a writer for the New Yorker. They had steamed dumplings and "Sichuan chicken that is about as Sichuan as I am"

places.name: Cafeteria a la BBQ
places.description: Tony ate with internationally renowned author Leonardo Padura. They had fried pork and plantains with flan made out in a cut down beer can.

places.name: Caribeño Restaurante (closed?)
places.description: Could not find this restaurant on the map. Tony had dorado, rice, beans, plantains, and rum.

places.name: Café de l'Abbaye
places.description: Tony and his best friend Eric Ripert ate at this outdoor cafe.

places.name: Le Petit Nice Passedat
places.description: Marseilles' only Michelin starred restaurant when this was filmed. They had bouillabaisse and a cheese course that Bourdain was obsessed with

places.name: Restaurant Fémina
places.description: Restaurant known for their couscous. Tony had the royale, which is served with veggies, merguez sausage, chicken, chickpeas, lamb, and meatballs.

places.name: Chez Georgiana (Closed)
places.description: Tony ate at this restaurant with Eric Ripert and many women in the restaurant business, who discussed how it is to be a female chef in the male-dominated business.

places.name: U Mio Paese
places.description: Tony and Eric had some fantastic charcuterie at a patio table outside of this charcuterie shop

places.name: The Champ De Mars
places.description: Tony and Eric had drinks at this bar with a local filmmaker & sports writer, Gilles Rof

places.name: Le Bar de la Plaine
places.description: Tony, Eric, and Gilles Rof (local sports writer) played foosball here

places.name: Ishikawa Dome
places.description: Tony watched bull sumo, or Tōgyū, while eating yakitori.

places.name: Urizun
places.description: Tony had pork belly (cooked with stock, heavily infused with bonito flakes), pork ears thinly sliced and dressed in rice-wine vinaigrette, slow-roasted ribs brined in sake and seasonings.

places.name: Gettouan
places.description: "A private home turned restaurant serving very traditional Okinawan dishes," Tony describes.Tony had "tundaabun"  or multiple bite-size portions served in a lacquered dish of "sliced squid; swordfish wrapped in seaweed and simmered in stock and fermented sake; dried sea snake, slow-simmered; burdock root wrapped in pork loin, slow-cooked in hot sauce; taro flash-fried and dressed with sugar and soy; pork shoulder dredged in black sesame, then steamed."

places.name: Makishi Market
places.description: A public food market where you can pick out the fish and they will cook it for you. Tony had snapper and parrot fish sashimi; sea grapes dressed in vinegar; gurukun (unofficial national fish of Okinawa) battered and deep-fried; porcupine fish, battered and deep-fried.

places.name: Lawson Kokusai Street kumoji
places.description: A mini market. Tony is obsessed with the egg salad sandwich. In the behind the scenes episode in season 12, the crew explained that the location they shot at ran out of egg salad sandwiches and there was a mad rush to find one at nearby Lawson stores.

places.name: The DOJO bar
places.description: Tony had drinks, horse meat and goat sashimi, pork belly, pickled pig’s ears, baked yam

places.name: Sinbad’s Pier 2 Restaurant (Closed)
places.description: Now closed, Tony had a meal here with John Birdsall, a food writer.

places.name: Ralph Gracie Jiu Jitsu
places.description: Tony trained here every morning while he was filming this episode. He trains at the sister school in NYC, and planned this entire episode just so he could train here.

places.name: Swan Oyster Depot
places.description: This was one of Tony's regular stops when he visited San Francisco. He had crab and oysters.

places.name: Trader Vic's Emeryville
places.description: Tony had ribs and mai tais with author Sean Wilsey.

places.name: 4505 Burgers & BBQ
places.description: Tony had a meal with Richie Nakano, a chef. They ordered the Presidential Platter, which is a sampler platter of brisket, hog, jalapeño and cheddar sausage, frankaroni (macaroni around a hot dog), ribs, chicken, beans, potato salad, coleslaw, bread, fries.

places.name: Miss Ollie's
places.description: Tony ate here with Bobby Seale, co-founder of the Black Panther Party. They ate skillet-fried chicken, rice and peas, greens

places.name: Juhu Beach Club
places.description: Tony met again with John Birdsall, author, to have Indian food. Chef Preeti Mistry described the dishes to them and discussed Indian food in America. They ate Manchurian cauliflower which Preeti described as “vegetable candy”, bhel puri (puffed-rice dish), chili paneer, yogurt-marinated chicken curry served on rice, Indian Scotch egg (the Scotch egg was invented in India).

places.name: Minato Japanese Restaurant
places.description: Tony and author Curt Fukuda discussed how the Japanese internments during WWII while eating tempura and Japanese curry tonkatsu.

places.name: COI
places.description: A two-Michelin starred restaurant. They had veggie burger, beet rose with yogurt and rose petal ice (signature dish), California sturgeon caviar, egg yolk poached in smoked oil, wild king salmon wrapped in yuba with charred cabbage in wild ginger sauce, beef tenderloin, black sesame mocha bun with strawberry kumquat.

places.name: Kategna Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with chef Marcus Samuelsson and his wife Maya. They had beyaynetu, a platter of various stews including goman (sauteed greens), shiro wat (chickpea stew), tikil gomen (sauteed white cabbage); all served with injera bread.

places.name: Mesi Grocery store/መጠጥ መሸጫ
places.description: Tony had shekla tibs, which are chunks of fried beef or lamb served in a clay pot with Ethiopian skateboarders and Marcus Samuelsson.

places.name: Local Tej Bet
places.description: Tony, Marcus, and Maya had tej, a light alcoholic drink made of fermented barley and honey

places.name: Wong Ah Wah Restaurant
places.description: After a very long plane ride, Tony had char kway teow (dish made with rice noodles), spicy pork noodles, black pepper crab, beer.

places.name: Restoran Seetharam Family Curry House
places.description: Tony ate here with a tattoo artist that did his ouroboros tattoo, Eddie David of Borneo Ink.

places.name: Aunty Aini's Garden Cafe
places.description: Tony sat with the chef and ate roast beef, poached egg, chicken rendang.

places.name: Choon Hui Cafe
places.description: Tony calls Sarawak laksa "a magical dish," it has chicken, prawn, coconut, chili, and noodles in a complex broth. “Best breakfast ever.”

places.name: Kuching
places.description: Tony returned to the small village of Kuching nearly a decade since his last visit. “When I first went up this river, I was sick with love—the bad kind, the fist around your heart kind. I ran far, but there was no escaping it. It followed me up river, all the way. That was 10 long years ago. … Yet here I am again, heading up to that same longhouse in the jungle.”

places.name: Poyraz Sahil Balık Restaurant
places.description: Tony had whole fried fish and an alcoholic beverage called raki

places.name: Karpi Trabzon Pidesi Ve Akçaabat Köftesi
places.description: Tony had Pide, the Turkish version of pizza, with ground meat, cheese, and onions

places.name: Mutfak Dili Ev Yemekleri (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Mutfak Dili Ev Yemekleri, where Tony had some traditional food.

places.name: Akar Lokantası
places.description: Tony's friend the taxi driver recommended this restaurant. They had Işkembe çorbası (tripe soup).

places.name: Princes Islands
places.description: Tony had a meal with a local poet on Princes Islands

places.name: Mikla
places.description: Tony had drinks at the rooftop bar of this restaurant/hotel.

places.name: The Griffon
places.description: Tony met with local chef Sean Brock to have beer, Jägermeister, Brock’s 1991 Stitzel-Weller bourbon

places.name: Waffle House
places.description: Chef Sean Brock introduced Tony to the wonders of the Waffle House, the only place for late night eats in the area. They had pecan waffles, patty melt cheeseburger, green salad with Thousand Island dressing, thin-cut pork chops, T-bone steak, hash browns

places.name: Charleston RiverDogs
places.description: A feeder team for the NY Yankees, Tony met with Bill Murray who is an owner of the team, to watch a game

places.name: Husk
places.description: One of Chef Sean Brock's restaurants, Tony and Billy Murray had a meal here.
