"Husk directly addresses Southern culinary traditions using the best of modern techniques, but always, ALWAYS respecting the originals and who made them," Tony says. They had old country ham made from a pig brought over from Spain in the 1500s called asabal, and aged 3 years. They also had oyster pie (a pre-Civil war recipe), shrimp & grits, hoppin john with Carolina gold rice, grilled whiting, suckling pig, creamed corn, and cornbread.

places.name: FIG
places.description: Tony ate with Chef Glen Roberts, owner of heirloom grain company Anson Mills. He ate in season soft-shell crab with pasta and shaved bottarga ("which frankly, I'd slit my best friend's throat for"), heirloom rice and peas with suckling pig and chicken confit over Carolina Gold rice

places.name: Atlantic Game & Tackle
places.description: Tony shopped here to prep for a turkey hunt.

places.name: Scott’s Bar-B-Que
places.description: Out in the middle of the country, Sean Brock brought Tony to this traditional whole hog pit barbecue, a place that has been in business for over 40 years. They do burn barrel barbecue that requires tending to all night long.

places.name: Mad Dog Motorcycle Club Philippines
places.description: Tony watched cover bands at this biker bar

places.name: Jollibee Bluebay Walk
places.description: Tony loves this local fast food joint, even though he said "I hate myself" as he was finishing his meal. He had Jolly Spaghetti (with hot dogs in it) and Chicken Joy with a brown sauce.

places.name: Pearl Garden Hotel
places.description: Tony crashed a holiday party here, the Seven Continents Travel & Tours corporate Christmas party. They had lechon, a whole pig roast pork that is traditional during the holidays.

places.name: Alvie
places.description: Tony had halo halo, which translates to "mix mix." It is an "icy, milky, technicolor concoction of sundry sweet mung beans, candied fruits, and gelatins." Also has banana, condensed milk, and sweet young coconut.

places.name: Super Six
places.description: Tony came here to eat his favorite Filipino street food, which he describes as "possibly the best thing you can ever eat with a cold beer." The dish he is talking about is sisig, a sizzling dish of pig face with a runny egg on top.

places.name: Oarhouse Pub of Manila
places.description: Tony met with journalist Mark Navales to have drinks at this bar.

places.name: Meal with a local
places.description: Tony had another meal with a local, the nanny of one of the show's directors, Erik Osterholm. She made kare kare, or oxtail soup. After living abroad for over 30 years, Aurora is finally back home living with her kids and grandchildren, although many still live abroad to send money home to support the family. They talked about the sacrifices Aurora and many other Filipinos make to send money for better lives for these poor families.

places.name: Old Town Ale House
places.description: Tony had a beer here with the owner of the bar. The owner also creates X-rated drawings of famous people and hangs them on the wall, including political figures, athletes, actors

places.name: Valois Restaurant
places.description: Cafeteria style restaurant where Tony ordered meatloaf and macaroni & cheese. He ate there with Bruce Cameron Elliott, who owns the Old Town Ale House. Bruce told Tony that he often saw Barack Obama having breakfast at this restaurant

places.name: The Second City
places.description: Legendary improv comedy theater where John Belushi, Gilda Radner, and others got their start.

places.name: Longman & Eagle
places.description: Tony ate here with comedian Paul Jurewicz. They had beef tartare, roasted bone marrow, tete de cochon with blue cheese and celery relish, confit of beef tripe, slow roasted cauliflower with caramelized onions and lentils.

places.name: Sze Chuan Cuisine
places.description: Tony ate here with Chef Stephanie Izard and Chef Peter Wong. They had pork dumplings in chili oil, mapo doufu, and fish hotpot.

places.name: Lunch at Lupe Fiasco's home
places.description: Tony had a meal at Chicago-based rapper Lupe Fiasco's house with his extended family. His mother made macaroni and cheese, collard greens, and other soul food.

places.name: Ricobene's
places.description: Tony met with musician, producer, and sound engineer Steve Albini. They had the famous breaded steak sandwich, which Tony said was "at least 4 pounds" and required lots of napkins.

places.name: Topo Gigio Ristorante
places.description: Tony had veal saltimbocca with Bruce Cameron Elliott and a pal from the Old Town Ale House whose nickname is Buzzkill. They discussed the Cubs and White Sox rivalry. His dining companions had scallops in pesto cream sauce and squid ink pasta with shrimp.

places.name: The Ocean Club
places.description: Not sure if this is the beach bar where Tony had some raki to start the day. It was a ocean side bar near where the boat dropped them off.

places.name: Souvlucky
places.description: Tony had lunch with local fisherman and discussed the economy. They had souvlaki, fries, and other Mediterranean goodies, and Mythos beer.

places.name: Bluefin divers Naxos Diving Center-Padi 5 Star Resort
places.description: Tony took a diving tour to see a sunken ship that sunk in 1982.

places.name: Taverna Platanos
places.description: Naxos style pork called rosto in a small village in the mountains.

places.name: Μαρίνα Νάξου / Naxos Dock
places.description: Tony had a meal on a boat with some fishermen. They had octopus, grilled salatouri (skate), marinated anchovies, grilled rofos (grouper), and mashed fava beans.

places.name: Medicine Tail Coulee
places.description: Tony met with Crow indian Kennard Real Bird, who ranches horses in the same location where General Custer "had the worst day of his life." There is also Indian relay racing, a dangerous sport involving jumping from moving horses.

places.name: Lydia's Supper Club
places.description: Tony had pickled beets, sweet potato salad, salami and cheese, side salad, pickled peppers, breadsticks, large scallops in white wine sauce, extra thick tenderloin of beef

places.name: Silver Dollar Saloon
places.description: Tony discussed the history of Butte as a union town with former congresswoman, Amanda Curtis

places.name: Orphan Boy Mine
places.description: Tony did some mining with miners.

places.name: Montana Pheasants Forever
places.description: Tony went hunting with a guide and stand-up comedian Joe Rogan. They cooked bird they shot over a fire, with a conservationist and a journalist from Field & Stream.

places.name: Mint Bar and Grill
places.description: Tony had beers at this bar that opened in the 1920s.

places.name: Meal at Jim Harrison's house
places.description: Tony ate with renowned poet Jim Harrison, who also wrote books about food. Jim's friend Dan cooked some spatchcock Hungarian partridge, liver loaf with elk meat, beets, quail in aspic, elk carpacchio, smoked trout.

places.name: Clouds Bar & Restaurant
places.description: Tony and Zamir met up at this bar on the top floor of the Radisson to drink chacha, a traditional Georgian moonshine

places.name: Peace Casino
places.description: Tony and Zamir gambled (and lost) at this casino. They played blackjack and roulette.

places.name: Dilber Gentlemen's Club
places.description: Tony and Zamir went to this strip club after they lost money at gambling.

places.name: Sazandari
places.description: "One of the city's older, more traditional joints" Tony said. Tony and Zamir are both hungover from the previous evening's shenanigans. They had a hangover cure, khashi which Zamir explained is made of beef broth from bones and joints. Tony did not think it worked. He did enjoy the chashushuli, "a stew of slow-cooked veal with onion and tomato, heavily seasoned with coriander, fennel, garlic, and chilies."

places.name: Gabriadze
places.description: Tony ate here with Paul Rimple, Tbilisi bureau chief for Culinary Backstreets. They had "salad with orange, almonds, and honey; grilled lamb ribs with pomegranate sauce; slices of fried eggplant wrapped around a walnut filling; chkmeruli, chicken slow-baked in an oven and then simmered in garlic and milk."

places.name: Sofia Melnikovas Fantastiuri Duqani
places.description: Tony had a meal with activist and journalist Tamara Chergoleishvili, founding director of Georgia’s Tabula Media. They had khinkali which Tony describes as “big, fat, juicy soup dumplings filled with spicy cumin-jacked minced beef in a hot, rich, potentially scalding broth”.

places.name: Culinarium (moved)
places.description: Tony ate here with chef Tekuna Gachechiladze, who is trying to modernize Georgian cuisine. I have also mapped the current location of this restaurant.

places.name: Culinarium Khasheria
places.description: New site of chef Tekuna Gachechiladze's restaurant.

places.name: Patara Khurvaleti
places.description: Tony headed out to this border agrarian town of only 9 families, to have a traditional supra. A supra is a Georgian feast. Tony ate with journalist Dima Bit-Suleiman, and Ushangi & Makvala Kokashvili. They had three varieties of khachapuri, a traditional cheese-filled bread, stuffed here with potato, beet leaves, or stewed cabbage; korma made from a roasted pig; homemade bread.

places.name: Pheasant's Tears Winery
places.description: The chef, Gia Rokashvili, uses local ingredients and serves the food with local wines. Tony had boiled beets in wild plum sauce, freshly foraged mushrooms with chili pepper, mint, and tarragon; baby lamb stewed with cumin, cooked with wild rice; fresh tonis puri, bread made in a tandoor-like clay oven. He ate with Keti Bochorishvili, former Deputy Minister of Economy and Sustainable Development, and Tamuna Gvaberidze, gallery owner.

places.name: Marche Kermel
places.description: Tony met at this bustling food market with NPR West Africa correspondent Ofeibea Quist-Arcton.

places.name: Huitres De Sokone
places.description: Tony met with a group of women (Fama Diof, photographer; Umi Nador, journalist and TV presenter; Minyel Tal, entrepreneur) to discuss gender roles in a mostly Muslim country. They ate clams, sea urchins, and thiof fish.

places.name: Restaurant le BAZOFF
places.description: Tony watched local musician Youssou N’Dour perform at this restaurant. They also ate beef mafe served with rice, a stew thickened with ground peanuts. Tony says, "there are similar preparations throughout West Africa, but the Senegalese version is particularly great."

places.name: Boulangerie SMBIS
places.description: Tony had a street food dish called ndambe, a spread of spicy lentils on a baguette. You buy the baguette from this bakery, then go to the woman on the street who has the lentils to spread on it.

places.name: Le jardin de l'Amitié
places.description: Tony met with a group of entrepreneurs to discuss the economy of Senegal to have drinks.

places.name: Dibiterie Le Mboté
places.description: Tony ate with rapper Djily Bagdad. They ate dibi, roasted meat served on paper with grilled onions and mustard.

places.name: Lac Rose
places.description: The pink lake, as it is called, is a site for harvesting salt, but is also known as the finish line "for a legendary trans-African endurance race." Tony sat with a driver as they did some offroading then had a meal together.

places.name: Brauerei zur Malzmühle
places.description: Tony met with Heinz Grüne, beer expert, to have some kolsch, a style of pale ale created in Cologne. They ate a halve hahn, "Gouda cheese on rye" and Mettbrötchen, or raw minced pork on a roll. They also had himmel und erde (blood sausage, fried onions, mashed potatoes, applesauce), and cured pork

places.name: Bei Oma Kleinmann
places.description: Tony met with Tracey Gudwin (friend and former producer for No Reservations) and Anke Lönne, a resident of Cologne. They had schnitzel, which Bourdain describes as “surfboard-sized slabs of veal and pork filled with many wonderful things, dredged in breadcrumbs and fried in magical, magical deep fat.”

places.name: Artistanbul Restaurant
places.description: Tony met with Melek Yaprak, aTurkish-German whose grandparents were among the first wave of Turkish immigrants in Cologne in this Turkish restaurant. They had Raki and mezze of "spicy mashed vegetables, tzatziki, hummus, beetroot and olive dip, fried eggplant, pastries with feta, meatballs with tomato sauce and mint"

places.name: Ox & Klee
places.description: Tony met with former rock star Irmin Schmidt of the Krautrock band Can. They had grilled scalllop with black salsify served with a consomme of burned hay, lemon gelee, and walnut oil; grilled lobster with a dashi of eel, marinated algae, fermented kale, and yuzu froth; braised ox cheeks with marrow, beet roots with mustard, gratin potatoes, black truffle.

places.name: De Fressbud
places.description: Tony met with the chef from Ox & Klee, Daniel Gottschlich and his friend Demetri to have grilled bratwurst with curry sauce.

places.name: Kleine Glocke
places.description: "A hangout for Cologne's artist community since the first World War." Tony ate sauerbraten (pot roast of horse meat) with chef René Stessl

places.name: Uerige
places.description: Serving beer and pork products since 1862. Tony met with Heinz and his friend Judith drank alt (a dark beer) and ate pickled eggs, cabbage and mettwurst, and spicy pork goulash with rye bread.

places.name: Bar Trattoria Celentano
places.description: Tony had a meal with Maurizio Arca, a Sardinian-German. They had wine, antipasti (carpaccio, tomatoes with pepper and parmesan, grilled zucchini, mortadella), spaghetti with white clam sauce

places.name: Habibi Köln
places.description: Tony met with Syrian immigrant Sakher Al Mohamad to talk about the unrest Cologne faced when middle eastern terrorists attacked Cologne residents on New Year's Eve 2015.

places.name: Don Carlos
places.description: Tony had a meal with Argentina's most famous chef, Francis Mallmann. They ate empanadas, grilled beef, and blood sausage

places.name: Los Talas del Entrerriano
places.description: Tony met with therapist Marianna and had parrilla, a traditional Argentine meal called achuras, including kidney, chorizo, morcilla.

places.name: Bar Sur
places.description: Tony met with a career server named Mario and had drinks and snacks.

places.name: Club GON
places.description: Tony came to this soccer club to drink beer and eat chorizo with Chef Soledad Nardelli, and watch kitchen staff of 2 rival restaurants play soccer at 1 am

places.name: Bún ốc Giang phố cổ
places.description: Tony started this Vietnam journey at this bún ốc restaurant in Hanoi's Old Quarter. Bún ốc is a snail noodle soup

places.name: Bánh Cuốn Nóng Kim Thoa
places.description: Tony met with Thao Griffiths, the country director of the Vietnam Foundation. They ate Banh cuon, a dish of ground pork with wood ear mushroom in a thin rice noodle roll that is dipped in broth.

places.name: Bún Chửi Ngô Sĩ Liên
places.description: Also known as "Cussing Noodles" due to the owner yelling at people, but is Tony's friend Ha Pham's favorite place to get bún chin zou, or as Tony describes, "a glorious steaming bowl of rice noodles with spicy chilies, a rich hearty porky broth with pig's knuckles and snout. It's the only item on the menu and it's good!"

places.name: Bia Hải Xồm
places.description: Tony met with his old friend Dinh Hoang Linh, his former Foreign Affairs Ministry minder. "Bia Hảirefers to the roadside joints where locals gather to enjoy keg dispensed, freshly brewed draft beer

places.name: Hạ Long Bay
places.description: Tony took a boat ride aboard the Emeraude to this bay, one of Vietnam's most visited destinations. "Hạ Long means 'where the dragon descends into the sea' and legend says that this is where a great dragon charged, protecting Vietnam from foreign invaders."

places.name: Bún chả Hương Liên
places.description: Tony met with President Obama (while he was in office) to have bún chả. It was Obama's first visit to Vietnam, and it reminded him of his childhood visits to Indonesia. They shared cold beers and bún chả, a traditional Vietnamese soup of grilled pork belly, pork patties, and noodles served in a broth of vinegar, sugar, fish sauce and served with chilies. Obama told a story about a city in Indonesia called Puncak where he would eat at a roadside restaurant overlooking the tea fields, with a river running through the restaurant. You could pick a fish and they'd fry it and serve it with a bed of rice. Obama said it was a simple meal but his favorite.

places.name: Dino's Bar
places.description: A burger joint that is also a hangout for Nashville musicians. They had burgers and hot fries (smothered with cheese and hot chicken flavor), which Tony called "country poutine."

places.name: Dandgure's Classic Southern Cooking
places.description: Tony went to this cafeteria style restaurant with Jesse Boyce (songwriter/producer),and ordered a combo popular in Nashville called the "meat & three" which includes a meat dish and 3 sides. Tony chose fried chicken with okra, corn, and mashed potatoes.

places.name: Bolton's Spicy Chicken & Fish
places.description: Tony tried the hottest version of Nashville Hot Chicken (and paid dearly for it later). He ate with Allison Mosshart of the band The Kills and her brother Matthew, a chef. He said he had a 12-hour "existentially awful experience" after eating the chicken.

places.name: Music City Cryo
places.description: Tony went here with Dean Fertita (guitarist and keyboard player for Queens of the Stone Age, musician with the Dead Weather). "Cryotherapy, the latest in muscle recovery treatments," explains Tony. Liquid nitrogen chills down the pod to -300 degrees which causes your body to kickstart anti-inflammatory proteins.

places.name: City House
places.description: Chef Tandy Wilson made pizza for Tony and Dean Fertita (guitarist and keyboard player for Queens of the Stone Age, musician with the Dead Weather).

places.name: Third Man Records
places.description: Tony went to The Blue Room to watch singer-songwriter Margo Price record directly to acetate at Jack White's studio.

places.name: The Patterson House
places.description: Cocktail bar with a mixologist. Upstairs there is a restaurant called Catbird Seat. Tony had a drink here before he ate a meal at the restaurant.

places.name: The Catbird Seat
places.description: 20-seat restaurant with a menu that changes nightly. They ate risotto with sunflower seeds instead of rice, topped with fermented sunchokes and garnished with sunflower sprouts; linguini with sea urchin and chili flakes, made with Japanese ingredients—wakame bucatini, yuzukoshō to represent the chili flakes, scallops dried and smoked and grated over the top; macaron and cookie.

places.name: Party at Alison Mosshart's house
places.description: Dead Weather and The Kills both performed at this house party. Tony and Matthew (Alison's chef brother) cooked a bunch of food including apple walnut salad, fried pork dumplings, pulled pork with red cabbage, brisket, and deviled eggs because "it ain't a party without deviled eggs."

places.name: Pinewood Social
places.description: The day after the big party at Alison's house, they went to get bloody marys and mimosas at this bowling alley with brunch food.

places.name: Xiaomingtang Dandantian Shuimian
places.description: For Tony and Eric Ripert's first place, they had dan dan tian shui mian.(spicy noodles) from this place.

places.name: Tiantian Restaurant
places.description: Tony and Eric ate at this sichuan restaurant, and Eric said the spice was "challenging" They had pickled chicken feet, la zi ji (spicy chicken), mapo tofu (Tony's favorite), tong jiao yu (Sichuan green-peppercorn fish)

places.name: Leshan Giant Buddha
places.description: Tony and Eric took a boat ride to see the largest stone buddha in the world, built in 713.

places.name: Lianglukou Hotpot
places.description: By the end of this meal of painfully spicy Sichuan hot pot (dog tail; tripe, or “thousand-layer stomach”; tofu skin, seaweed, various meats and fishes, intestines), Eric said he couldn't think anymore (because of the spice). Tony enjoyed the meal and watching Eric squirm.

places.name: Yu Zhilan Restaurant (Closed?)
places.description: Not sure if this restaurant is still here. Tony and Eric met with Fuchsia Dunlop, a James Beard award-winning writer and cook who specializes in Chinese cuisine. They had "lily buds scented with rosebuds, thinly sliced beef shank with chili oil, tea tree mushrooms blanched and lightly stir-fried, prawns served cold with homemade noodles dressed with natural vegetable extracts, noodles hand-cut with duck-egg yolks, braised sea cucumber."

places.name: Qingshui River
places.description: Tony and Eric took a train out to Long Teng Dong Jie to eat a meal with Chen Zhong, a filmmaker.

places.name: St John Restaurant
places.description: Tony ate here with food critic Jay Rayner. Tony describes how highly he regards this restaurant, "The food that I yearn for is food that I react to in an entirely emotional way. I'm looking for a suspension of logic and reason. This is something that I got here from the beginning." They had "roast bone marrow with parsley and caper salad, sourdough made here" along with "pickled calves' tripe with radish, shaved carrot, and watercress; skate poached in court-bouillon, with fennel in green sauce of fresh parsley, herbs, and anchovies; pan seared kidney in savory sauce served on toast; pig’s head and potato pie."

places.name: Princess Victoria
places.description: Tony met with legendary cookbook author Nigella Lawson to have some Scotch eggs, whitebait ("tiny baby herring lightly battered whole and fried, tossed with a little bit of lemon juice and salt"), fries, and Guinness.

places.name: Rudloe Arms Hotel By Marco Pierre White
places.description: An inn and a restaurant. Tony and Chef Marco Pierre White had "a pan-roasted filet of beef with escargot; pig's trotter a la Pierre Koffman. The chef raises the pigs on the grounds of the inn.

places.name: Pepper & Spice Restaurant London
places.description: Tony met with Jamie Hince, "half the creative alliance that makes up the band The Kills." This is Jamie's "favorite local joint for Caribbean food"

places.name: Chequers Inn
places.description: "Artist, author, icon, Ralph Steadman continues to make art every day. He was the visual expression of Hunter S. Thompson's work." Tony met with Steadman at this restaurant/pub for lunch. "Runny egg wrapped in Parma ham and breadcrumbs, prawn cocktail, fish & chips with mushy peas, steak & stilton pie"

places.name: Marlboro
places.description: Tony met with Chef Marco Pierre for deep fried haddock, mushy peas, and a chip butty.

places.name: Rochelle Canteen
places.description: Tony met with the chef, Margo Henderson. They had wine, peas in their pods, radishes, vitello tonnato, Welsh lamb chops with lentils and green sauce.

places.name: The Hunter S
places.description: Tony had some Guinness.

places.name: Breakfast at Nigella's house
places.description: Tony was hungover so Nigella cooked Eggs in Purgatory for him, bread toasted in beef fat, served with runny eggs and spicy chili tomato sauce.

places.name: Margaret Long Wisdom High School
places.description: Tony visited this high school when it was called Robert E. Lee Senior High School. It is the most ethnically diverse school in Houston, with 80% of students speaking a language other than English as their first language.

places.name: Burns Original BBQ
places.description: Tony had not been back in 15 years. He ordered "the torpedo-sized baked potato filled with cheddar cheese and chopped barbecue beef, and homemade link sausage. Slow cooked pork ribs, big ass beef ribs, and brisket."

places.name: The Point
places.description: The Vietnamese owners fulfill a niche of the community, serving Vietnamese and Mexican food. Pho (brisket, eye round, meatball, tripe, and tendon); shrimp ceviche; tacos with eggs, jalapenos, tomatoes.

places.name: Acapulco Ballroom & Bridal Shop
places.description: Tony crashed a Quinceañera held here and talked with the owners about the "quince" culture in the Houston area.

places.name: La Guadalupana Bakery & Cafe
places.description: A traditional Mexican and Tex-Mex restaurant. Tony ate Machaca Nortena ("marinated beef that's been pounded, rubbed with spices, then dried and shredded") and Migas (eggs mixed with fried and chopped corn tortillas).

places.name: Plant It Forward (Warehouse)
places.description: Plant It Forward farmers Gertrude and Albert Lombo and Gimoule and Constant Ngouala hosted a meal with Tony and other friends. They had "slow-cooked stew of sausage, shrimp, dried mackerel, and Malabar spinach over fufu; then Texas beef brochettes marinated in chilies and cilantro  and Maggi bouillon with a ratatouille made from produce grown right here."

places.name: Sardar Patel Stadium
places.description: Tony met with Kuldeep Patel, league president of the Houston Indian Cricket Club, and other local cricket players. They watched a game and then ate tandoori chicken, curried goat, and potato masala dosas.

places.name: Masa
places.description: America's most expensive restaurant, it costs around $600 per person. It is run by Masa Takayama, America's most respected sushi chef. Tony traveled Japan with Masa.

places.name: Ōmichō Market
places.description: Tony and Masa ate fresh seafood at this market. They had sea urchin, kanogani (snow crab), grilled eel livers, and giant oysters "as big as freakin' clown shoes"

places.name: Higashi Chaya District
places.description: Tony and Masa watched a geiko perform at Fujinoya Tea House (could not find exact location on map).

places.name: Yamanoo
places.description: Traditional kaiseki restaurant that dates back to 1890. Menu changes as seasonal ingredients become available. ""Preserved in a tightly wrapped bamboo leaf, intended to resemble a sword, sea bream served chimaki-style over rice slightly sweetened by vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, and sesame... Grilled rockfish made Horaku style, steamed in smoking wormwood. Bamboo shoots and wagyu beef prepared tableside with soy sauce and mirin. Today's sashimi course is cod, salted and pressed between leaves of kelp for 2 hours, coated with sake-cured codfish roe. Next to it, flounder, its skin gently rubbed with grilled tomato the day before. Finally, a super luxurious clam hot pot: rice is steamed with clam and a bonito broth, then topped with plump torigai plum and manju clams, as well as creamy sea urchin and a Japanese broccolini blossom for good measure. Simple, perfect."

places.name: Yamanaka Onsen
places.description: Tony described this area as "unlike anyplace I've ever been in Japan." Masa's good friend and pottery mentor, Haruo Konishi has a family hideaway here. They made iwana (char) caught that day in a mountain stream, Hokkaido scallops, wild Japanese boar, grilled fukinoto, and soba noodles with duck, green onion, and dashi sauce.

places.name: Ginza Sushi Ko Honten
places.description: One of the BEST sushi places in the world. Masa learned here. They had seared horse mackerel over green onion and ginger, maguro (bluefin tuna), Japanese tiger prawn, octopus, fluke sashimi, striped jack, anago (sea eel) rolled in seaweed, and tamagoyaki (breaded shrimp eggs and fish paste)

places.name: Nasushiobara
places.description: The rural town where Masa grew up. Tony and Masa visited his family (mom and daughter). His mother made motsuni, "a slow simmered stew of pork tripe, konnyaku, daikon, green onions, and miso," sashimi with freshly grated wasabi, uni, and otoro (tuna).

places.name: Sugimura (Closed?)
places.description: Masa's brother Kazuo had a restaurant in Nasushiobara called Sugimura. Could not find it on the map. They had "kimoyaki, eel liver dipped in a sauce of sake, soy, and mirin, and grilled low and slow over charcoal... insanely delicious custard of eel and egg, jacked with bean curd, bonito broth, and kelp... hitsumabushi, eel steamed, dressed, and grilled, over rice."

places.name: Kita Onsen（株）北温泉旅館
places.description: Tony met with Masa's high school friends and had a soak in the hot spring, then ate sukiyaki (meat slowly cooked, tableside, in a broth), Tochigi beef with maitake (type of mushroom), and home-brewed sake

places.name: Mercado Central de Belo Horizonte
places.description: Tony met with chef Leo Paixão, who trained under Joel Robuchon and now runs Glouton. They tried all sorts of fruits and veggies only found in Brazil.

places.name: Bar Da Lora
places.description: Tony and Leon met with Ivo Faria, the “godfather” of mineiro cuisine at this restaurant inside the Mercado Central. They had beer, liver with jiló (a veggie like eggplant, only green and only edible when ripe).

places.name: Bar Do Careca
places.description: A boteco, or neighborhood restaurant that serves beer. Tony met with Eduardo Maya, “self-taught gastronome,” and Luiz Otavio, a beverage tycoon who specializes in cachaça at Cachaça Vale Verde. They had "ox tongue cooked with basil, mint, and pepper" and "feijão tropeiro: beans, manioc flour smoked in cured meats" and fresh eggs.

places.name: Inhotim Museum
places.description: Deep in the jungle, this contemporary art museum built by an eccentric visionary.

places.name: Birosca S2
places.description: Tony met with a group of women chefs to eat at this restaurant, including Bruna Martins, who owns the restaurant. Bruna only hires women to work there. They had "turkey neck, braised with butter and garlic, served with white beans, topped with kale and pork belly... ox hump pot-roast, served with mashed potatoes and farofa." While there, they had a scare when a drunk man in a stolen car was seen with a gun. The cameraman and producer dove on top of Tony to protect him, to which he said, "you know, you're NOT the secret service!"

places.name: Nono - O Rei do Caldo de Mocotó
places.description: Open very late into the night, this place serves mocotó, "the ultimate in broke-ass, drunk-ass peasant food. Slow-cooked cow’s foot and/ or other bits, tender and tasty, and believe me, one of my favorites, especially at this hour. We opt for the elite version, served with raw quail egg."

places.name: Osteria dal 1931
places.description: Tony ate by himself here, he had Chianti, prosciutto and artichoke hearts in olive oil, ravioli.

places.name: Gladiators
places.description: Tony and Asia Argento had pasta for dinner ringside at a boxing match.

places.name: L'Angolo degli amici (Closed)
places.description: Former site of this restaurant, which translated to Friends' Corner.

places.name: Simply Market
places.description: Tony and Asia went shopping here for a lunch her sister prepared for them. They bought fava beans, cheese, and other goodies.

places.name: Trattoria Morgana
places.description: Tony had a meal with Abel Ferrara, film director known for Kings of New York and Bad Lieutenant, who now lives in Rome with his family. They had Roman pastas, snails, and  artichokes.

places.name: Bar Caffetteria
places.description: Tony had espresso and a bomba, a type of Italian donut, for breakfast.

places.name: Trattoria Settimio
places.description: The restaurant that Tony and Asia visited in Rome that Tony refused to disclose the name of.

places.name: Palazzo dei Congressi
places.description: Tony and Asia visited this huge structure, which was built originally to be a symbol of fascism.

places.name: Gish Bac
places.description: "Serves specialties from the city of Tlacolula de Matamoros in Oaxaca, Mexico," says Tony. He visited this place with UCLA Chicano Studies professor, Raul Hinojosa-Ojeda. They had black mole and barbacoa.

places.name: La Reyna
places.description: This is a cart that sells mulitas, "not quite a taco, not a quesadilla. More of a taco sandwich. First the tortilla goes on the grill, then marinated meat, cheese, then another tortilla. Flip and serve."

places.name: Cielito Lindo
places.description: Tony met comedian Al Madrigal and editor of OC Weekly, Gustavo Arellano. They went to this place to get some taquitos, "rolled up, fried, smothered in avocado sauce, topped with chile guero, garlic, tomatillo, and cilantro." Also, a burrito with beans with machaca, spicy shredded beef, sauce, and cheese.

places.name: Trejo's Cantina - Hollywood
places.description: Actor Danny Trejo's restaurant. Tony had an assortment of tacos just before they had their grand opening: "jidori chicken with chipotle cream sauce, some charred branzino with summer squash and sauteed poblano peppers, and crispy pork tacos with black garlic mole and some fat sacks of uni, and lots of healthy greens."

places.name: Mariscos Chente
places.description: Tony had camarones borrachos (Drunken Shrimp) with 3 MMA fighters: Gilbert Melendez, Nick Diaz, and Nate Diaz.

places.name: Broken Spanish
places.description: Fancy Mexican restaurant by Chef Ray Garcia. Chicharron cooked souvide, slow cooked lamb-neck tamales with oyster mushrooms and queso oaxaca, Okinawan sweet potato filled with pork, topped with piloncillo syrup with bread with foie gras and piloncillo.

places.name: Caña Rum Bar
places.description: Tony had drinks with an ex-cop named Alex Salazar that admits he crossed lines and did bad things when he was a street cop.

places.name: Tacos Indiana
places.description: A taco truck serving carne asada, the slow grilled marinated beef classic served as a burrito with rice and beans, or simply on a tortilla as a taco. Tacos lengua, beef tongue braised with garlic and onions. And tacos al pastor, stacked guajillo chili rubbed pork shoulder slices with your choice of toppings."

places.name: Ganbara
places.description: Tony went on a poteo (bar crawl) with the famous chef Juan Mari Arzak and his daughter Elena. They ate a lot of pintxos and drank beer. They did not specify the places they visited except for this one, because this was Tony's favorite place to get pintxos, and the first place someone brought him to when he first visited the Basque area. Iberico ham with mushrooms on toast, crab tartlets, seared wild mushrooms and foie gras with egg yolk (house specialty, and Tony's favorite)

places.name: Albaola Itsas Kultur Faktoria
places.description: Xabier Agote, Shipwright and founder of the Basque Maritime Museum, is working here to built a 16th century style Basque galleon.

places.name: Elkano Kalea
places.description: Tony and Xabier had Rock prawn (head and body cooked separately—head grilled and body served semi-ceviche), grilled squid with onion-green pepper sauce, cocochas (hake fish jowls or chins), and grilled turbot (this is the dish people travel to this restaurant to try)

places.name: Arzak
places.description: One of Tony's favorite restaurants in the world. Tony said this restaurant made him feel like he was home.

places.name: Ostalapia
places.description: Tony had a meal with Miray Girt, who was born in Spanish Basque country but raised in France.They discussed the differences between French and Spanish Basque foods. They had "ventrèche de thon, or tuna belly with tomato carpaccio; pigeon, roasted and then flambéed, served with a terrine or pâté of the same bird."

places.name: Sociedad Aizepe
places.description: Tony met with a group at this place. They discussed "txokos" or "private and historically all-male gastronomical societies, though that gender rule has changed with the times." The menu for the evening: "diced raw tuna, grilled prawns with green sauce" to start, then "for the main, fried hake cooked with txakoli and steamed clams."

places.name: Asador Etxebarri
places.description: This place is in the specialist category - grill master in the Basque. Voted 3rd best restaurant in the world. This place is in the specialist category - grill master in the Basque. Voted 3rd best restaurant in the world. Tony said he had one of the best meals of his life here, definitely unlike anything he'd ever eaten.

places.name: Casa Urola
places.description: Tony ate here with Olatz González Abrisketa, a documentarian and professor of social anthropology at the University of the Basque Country. "Seared mushrooms with egg yolk and pine nuts... grilled tuna with marmitako sauce, a reference to a classic fishermen's stew;  peas in a consommé of Ibérico ham," and squid with a white bean cream.

places.name: Phosy Market
places.description: Tony met with James Syhabout, a chef and restaurateur behind two-Michelin star restaurant Commis, in San Francisco. They had a bowl of khao piak sen, or Lao noodle soup, which is James' first dish he seeks out once he gets off the plane. The dish includes crispy sliced pork, blood cake, meat broth.

places.name: Boun Ork Phansa
places.description: Tony visited during this Buddhist event that marks the end of Buddhist lent. There is a fire lantern festival, the symbolic casting away of one's sins. Tony watched a boat race on the Mekong River.

places.name: Villa Ban Lao Hotel
places.description: "During the Secret War, [this hotel] was the offices for USAID, America's foreign aid mission," says Tony. He had lunch here with the hotel owner, Mama Vahn, who made the meal, "an elaborate spread of dishes whose recipes are left over from imperial times." They had lon song, a fish from the Mekong River, cooked with shallots, garlic, chili, coconut milk, and pork; river crab, fish with pork grilled in banana leaves, ginger sauce, pork skin, and sticky rice.

places.name: UXO Lao
places.description: Tony met with workers from this organization tasked with finding unexploded objects left behind by American forces during the Secret War and safely blowing them up. Only 0.5% of the country has been cleared of unexploded devices. Kids and adults have been maimed and dismembered by these bombs strewn about the countryside.

places.name: Roosevelt Av - Jackson Heights
places.description: Take the 7 train and get off at Roosevelt Ave. to find lots of awesome food stands. One place sells Ecuadorian food right at the exit to the subway, such as hornado, or "roast pork with sublime crispy skin straight from Heaven, morcilla, or blood sausage with potato cakes." The cart is called La Esquina de la Caserita.

places.name: Yu Garden Dumplings (Closed)
places.description: Tony met with Himanshu Suri, rapper and member of the Swet Shop Boys hip hop group, who is also known as Heems. They had an assortment of dumplings at this restaurant, which is now permanently closed.

places.name: Geo Si Gi
places.description: For great Chinese food in Flushing. Tony met with chefs Josh Smookler and Cosme Aguilar. They had galbi, or "short ribs marinated in soy, garlic, and sesame oil, then grilled." and gam ja tang, a soup made from pork neck bones.

places.name: Neir's Tavern
places.description: Tony had a beer with Loycent “Loy” Gordon, the owner of the tavern, and New York City Fire Department lieutenant. The movie "Goodfellas" filmed scenes in this bar.

places.name: Lhasa Fast Food
places.description: Tony met with Hemms and his friend, attorney, and community organizer, Ali Najmi. They had thentuk, which is a hand torn noodle soup with chilies and ginger. Also, shaptra or chili-fried beef made of thinly sliced beef cooked with chili oil, soy, and Sichuan peppercorns; and sha momo, or steamed beef dumplings.

places.name: Brisas Del Mar
places.description: Tony had a meal with Josmar Trujillo, a writer and activist. They did not say what they had but it involved rice and beans and some kind of grilled meat.

places.name: Aqueduct Racetrack
places.description: Tony met with his friend and author Laurie Woolever, who co-wrote his cookbook Appetites with him. They bet on some horses and ate Jamaican beef patties.

places.name: Africana
places.description: A Nigerian restaurant in Jamaica Queens, with chef Blessing Osagiede running the place. Tony ate here with Sarah K. Khan, a food & culture writer, filmmaker, and photographer.

places.name: McMurdo Station
places.description: Largest US base on Antarctica, and a tight knit community of researchers, scientists, carpenters, pilots, machine operators, and others who run the station.

places.name: Galley
places.description: Tony had a meal with 2 women heavy machine operators.

places.name: Mount Lister
places.description: Tony went on a helicopter ride with pilot Ryan Skorecki, who flies scientists around the continent to remote areas. He pointed out Mount Lister from the sky.

places.name: Mount Erebus
places.description: On the helicopter ride, they also saw Mount Erebus, the southernmost active volcano on Earth.

places.name: Cape Royds Adélie Penguin Rookery
places.description: Tony visited this penguin colony of 2000 breeding pairs. He talked with marine ecologists Dr. David Ainley, who has been studying the colony for 20 years.

places.name: Lake Hoare
places.description: One of the most oldest and legendary base camps. Everything comes in by helicopter. The manager at the camp is said to be the best cook on the continent. She made grilled pork tenderloin, grilled mahi sticks, shrimp marinated in chili sauce, homemade sourdough bread, roasted beet salad, edamame salad, roasted vegetables.

places.name: South Pole
places.description: Tony visited the Polar Plateau. It is 9000 feet above sea level. The altitude and extreme wind is incredibly harsh, dropping to below 50F. The base has the feel of a space station.

places.name: Mutrah Souq
places.description: Tony and Aisha Stoby, PhD candidate of Middle Eastern art, strolled through this market and talked about the culture of Oman.

places.name: Mutrah Corniche
places.description: "I love that word, corniche. I love any city with a corniche," said Tony.

places.name: Bait Al Luban Restaurant
places.description: "Dishes like pakora and kachori which both came back from India, chapati from East Africa, and shuwa, Oman's classic special event dish.... they slather a goat with a spicy paste consisting of cumin, coriander, red pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, and nutmeg. Then wrap the meat in palm or banana leaves, dig a hole, throw in some meat, and cover it up and leave underground for a day or two over hot coals."

places.name: Al Hamra
places.description: A catering business run by women whose profits go to funding children's education in rural areas. They cooked a meal of "kabuli laham, slow cooked goat in a rice pilaf scented with star anise. Musanif djaj, a local specialty, are pan seared dumplings stuffed with chicken, pepper, ginger, turmeric, and onions. And of course there's Omani bread with honey."

places.name: Sharqiya Sands
places.description: Tony camped out here in the desert and ate a meal with locals. They cooked the meal on the fire.

places.name: Queen's Park Savannah
places.description: Tony had fried snapper here at a place with many street food stands.

places.name: Ali's Doubles
places.description: Everyone tells Tony he needs to try doubles. "Doubles are a Caribbean take on the Indian chana bhatura, two floppy, tender pieces of soft, Indian-style bread loaded with a wet heap of curried chickpeas, pepper sauce, and mango."

places.name: A Cozinha Do Martinho
places.description: Tony had fried sardines, Tripe Porto-style, and wine.

places.name: Real Companhia Velha
places.description: Tony tried port and assorted cheeses, bread, pumpkin jam.

places.name: Cervejaria Gazela - O Original Cachorrinho
places.description: Tony had cachorros here, made of toasted bread, fresh pork sausage, cheese, and a spicy sauce. And fries

places.name: Marisqueira Antiga
places.description: Tony had an enormous platter of seafood consisting of "coastal shrimp, local oysters and crab, sea urchin, langoustine, gooseneck barnacles, whelks, and clams, and a salt-baked sea bass."

places.name: Café Restaurante O Afonso
places.description: Tony had a francesinha (a sandwich made of sliced bread, steak, sausage, linguiça, cooked in a sauce of beer, port wine, spice, tomato, and a lot of cheese melted on top).

places.name: 545 Whampoa Prawn Noodle
places.description: Tony met with KF Seetoh, his guide in Singapore, "the guru behind the Makansutra guides and an advocate for the kind of democratic, affordable, casual chaos of the hawker world." They had prawn mi from this stand.

places.name: Sabar Menanti II
places.description: Tony met with Singaporean- Malay entertainer and producer Najip Ali for Lontong (rice cake, coconut gravy, vegetables and hardboiled egg) and Mee Siam (fried rice noodles with chili paste, prawns, bean sprouts, and a spicy sweet and sour gravy, fried bean curd, and egg).

places.name: Lorong 29 Geylang
places.description: Tony had a meal with Sadir Varaket, Melanie Chan, and Tanya Andoor. They had fried oyster omelets and fried prawns with chili paste; bak chor mi, a fish ball noodle soup; and beef satay.

places.name: Guan Hoe Soon Restaurant
places.description: "The oldest Peranakan restaurant in Singapore, predating independence." They had a Laotian dish called ikam asam nana, a sour fish curry with pineapple; itek sio, a duck braised in tamarind and coriander; and okra sautéed with sambal.

places.name: Keng Eng Kee Seafood
places.description: Tony had a meal with the family who runs this restaurant. They had pig trotters with black vinegar (and ginger, fried chilies, and asam), Hainan mixed vegetables (cabbage and glass noodles stir fried with bean curd, black mushrooms, pig skin, and shrimp).

places.name: Outram Park Fried Kway Teow Mee
places.description: One of Tony's favorite things to eat in Singapore is char kway teow. "Ordinarily this is a breakfast," Tony explains. "I eat it every time I'm in Singapore." It is made of crispy lard bits, cockles, shrimp paste, and noodles.

places.name: Aiguille des Grands Montets
places.description: Tony and Eric Ripert went skiing here in the French Alps, which Tony describes as a place he fears, but is allowing Eric to have "payback" for the torture Eric endured in Sichuan China.

places.name: Restaurant Plan Joran
places.description: While skiing in the French Alps, Tony and Eric Ripert stopped here. They had pan-seared foie gras, seared veal loin with a wild mushroom sauce and vegetable medley, a cheese course.

places.name: Crémerie du Glacier
places.description: "A special place at the bottom of the mountain," describes Tony. "croute with morilles, and cream, lots of cream....farcante: a big, heavy, albeit thoroughly delicious loaf of potato, bacon, dried fruit, and cream slowly steamed in a bain marie."

places.name: Hôtel du Buet
places.description: Time for more cheese. Tony and Eric had raclette and fondue here.

places.name: Boucherie du Mont Blanc - Chamonix (Francia)
places.description: Eric Ripert met with Maurice Guillot, Eric's mentor at this butcher/cheese shop. They bought beef tenderloin to make steak au poivre, tomme de chevre, and saucisson.

places.name: ChaChaCha
places.description: Eric Ripert and mentor Maurice Guillot went to this wine & spirits store to purchase champagne, white burgundy, and cognac.

places.name: Peloton de gendarmerie de haute montagne (PGHM)
places.description: Eric's dream as a kid was to work as a park ranger, so they met with a lady named Sonia Popoff who does this for a living. "She's who you call when things are really bad," says Tony. They rescue skiiers who injure themselves on the mountain.

places.name: Farmhouse Maison Rosset
places.description: Tony hopped the border to Italy because he craved pasta in the Aoeste Valley. They had gnocchi with beet root fondue, ravioli with tomato sauce, creamy polenta with braised beef. Tony, Eric, and host played feur lane, a game where "you try to hit that tiny polished stone with that stick as far as you can."

places.name: Rue80
places.description: Tony had drinks with Shina Peller, owner of Club Quilox, (a music venue) and Banky Wellington, record label owner

places.name: Iya-Eba Restaurant & Bar
places.description: Tony had spicy pepper soup with journalist  Kadaria Ahmed

places.name: Computer Village
places.description: Tony met with an economics expert to talk about the financial ecosystem of this marketplace. Young people learn to take apart and fix phones for a living.

places.name: Stellas Kitchen
places.description: Tony visited this restaurant to have pounded yams, egusi soup, "a stew of goat meat, melon seeds, fish stock, and chilies."

places.name: New Afrika Shrine
places.description: Tony met the owner of this musical venue

places.name: Central Mosque - Hausa Community Agege
places.description: Tony met with His Royal Highness, Alhaji Musa Muhammadu Dogonkadai, Sarkin Hausawan Agege. They had masa (griddle cakes made from sticky rice, sugar, potash—a mineral-rich salt), fura (millet and milk compote), danwake (bean-flour dumplings served with tomatoes), moringa salad, kuli-kuli powder (ground peanut).

places.name: Yakoyo Food Restaurant
places.description: Traditional Nigerian foods such as ewedu (jute leaf) soup with crayfish, chilies, and locust beans; jollof rice (cooked in tomato sauce); goat; pounded yam.

places.name: Andela Nigeria
places.description: Tony visited this training program where people are taught coding and software engineering.

places.name: Bloomfield Park
places.description: Tony played bocce ball and had a picnic of sausage and peppers and got beat by a 103-year old.

places.name: Squirrel Hill Cafe
places.description: Tony had beer and a burger with author Stewart O'Nan.

places.name: Pittsburgh
places.description: Tony had "racks of pork rib, grilled hearts of escarole and turnips, a sauce made from the pork drippings, and four types of sausage" with Chefs Justin Severeno and Sonja Finn.

places.name: Grandma B's
places.description: Tony had a sandwich and fries with Sala Udin, Hill District community leader, former councilman.

places.name: Kelly's Bar & Lounge
places.description: Tony had beer, kielbasa, sauerkraut, pierogies, and mac & cheese with wrestlers Brittany Baker and Adam Cole

places.name: Jozsa Corner
places.description: "More like a house party with food," than a restaurant, says Tony. They had chicken paprikash, lángos (Hungarian fried bread), kolbasz (Hungarian sausage), cucumber salad.

places.name: Spoon (Closed)
places.description: Tony had a meal here with the chef.

places.name: Hidy's Cafe
places.description: Tony met with filmmaker Tony Buba to have drinks and discuss the economy of Braddock's blue collar neighborhood.

places.name: Superior Motors
places.description: Tony had a meal with John Fetterman, mayor of Braddock; Gisele Fetterman, who runs a nonprofit to help those in need in their community; Franco Harris, four-time Super Bowl champion; and Chef Kevin Sousa, restaurateur behind Superior Motors. They had Lake Erie walleye pike, new potatoes, fish cracklin, and fresh herbs; beef short ribs on milkweed buds and overwintered sunchoke chips; wine.

places.name: New Alexandria Lions Club Demolition Derby
places.description: Tony had beer with Brooke Davis, demolition driver, and Chris Quenzler Jr., mechanic

places.name: Galle Face Green
places.description: "This is our Central Park," Dharshan Munidasa says (a chef and TV personality). Tony and Dharshan had street food here.

places.name: Nelum Kole Restaurant
places.description: Tony had lunch at this local rice and curry joint with Kumar Lopez, CEO of Sri Lanka Press Institute, a news trade organization. They had chicken curry, spicy potatoes, beets, and rice.

places.name: Wellawatte Railway Station
places.description: Tony took a 10-hour train ride to Jaffna.

places.name: Jaffna Railway Station
places.description: Tony got off the train here in Jaffna, a coastal city "as far North as you can go before you hit India."

places.name: Kali Kovil
places.description: Tony visited during the Madai Festival for the Tamil people who are praying to Kali, a Hindu goddess.

places.name: Jaffna Beach
places.description: Tony’s last meal in Sri Lanka is with local fishermen. They had grouper and squid with beer.

places.name: Esquina El Watusi
places.description: Tony had drinks with Hermes Ayala, freelance journalist for outlets including Vice and HuffPost; Laura Moscoso, journalist specializing in data visualization and multimedia.

places.name: Casa Vieja
places.description: Tony had a meal with Liza Fournier Cordova, a teacher. They had corn fritters with ham; plantain soup; pastel al caldero, or "pork marinated in bitter orange, taro root, green plantains, squash, and garbanzo beans, slow cooked"; morcilla (blood sausage).

places.name: El Trasmayo
places.description: Tony met with chef and entrepreneur Pedro Álvarez Cortez at this crab shack. They had crab, coconut arepas, and beer.

places.name: Salvemos a Playuela
places.description: Tony met with activist "Pocho" to have  a feast of Sancocho, a beef stew with yuca, plantains; fresh-caught grouper and red snapper.

places.name: El Rancho de Apa
places.description: Tony had slow roasted suckling pig with ranch owner Apa Ramos. They also had gandinga, a stew made from pig heart, liver, and kidney.

places.name: Puerto de Vieques
places.description: Tony had a meal on a boat to Vieques. They ate salted cod marinated in coconut milk and seared, root vegetables, escabeche, and avocado sala

places.name: Pacific Inn Pub
places.description: Tony had fish & chips with Astra Elane and Dustin Patterson, members of local band The Gods Themselves.

places.name: Revel
places.description: Tony ate at this Korean restaurant with John Cook and Todd Bishop, co-founders of tech news website GeekWire. They had: "cilantro noodles with yu choy, and thinly sliced flat iron steak; kimchi pancakes with braised pork belly and bean sprouts; marinated short rib rice bowl with house sambal daikon, kimchi, and egg; and short rib dumplings topped with pickled shallot and scallion salad."

places.name: Emerald Haze Cannabis Emporium
places.description: Tony went to this weed dispensary to buy some pot, since it is legal in the state of Washington

places.name: The Hollingsworth Cannabis Company
places.description: A marijuana farm that supplies some of the dispensaries

places.name: Mamnoon
places.description: Tony had a meal with the Hollingsworths, from the cannabis farm. They had Lebanese food here: "baharat spiced beef and lamb kabobs, fried cauliflower with tahini, a summer fatteh with local squash and charred tomatoes topped with sizzling brown butter and pine nuts; sweet and sour rice wrapped in grape leaves; hummus."

places.name: Modernist Cuisine
places.description: Tony visited this cooking lab and met with Nathan Myhrvold, author of a book Modernist Cuisine. They had baba au rhum in a jar, and a bagel.

places.name: Vashon Island
places.description: Tony ate with local chefs. They had a "clam bake with dungeness crab, spot prawns, corn, and potatoes, covered in kelp and steamed together under burlap."

places.name: Taylor Shellfish Oyster Bar
places.description: Tony ate with a group. They had lots of oysters; flash-fried whole local smelt served with pepperoncini sambal aioli; local dungeness crab, chilled and cooked with pickled ginger sauce.

places.name: North Star Diner & Shanghai Room
places.description: Tony met with retired Seattle homicide detective Mike Ciesynski to discuss serial killers. They ordered food from North Star Diner: crab, bacon, and melted cheese sandwich

places.name: Ocho
places.description: Tony met with rock star Mark Lanegan (from Queens of the Stone Age, and also the person who wrote the theme show of Parts Unknown). They had tapas and drinks.

places.name: Ricciolandia
places.description: Tony had uni pasta and beer.

places.name: Palazzo Margherita Hotel
places.description: Tony had a meal with Francis Ford Coppola. They had lampascioni, or scored and fried hyacinth bulbs; braciole di cotenna, or stuffed pig skin rolls; lamb’s head casserole, fresh fruit, assorted wines.

places.name: Masseria Spina Resort
places.description: Tony had a pasta dinner here with Asia Argento.

places.name: Matera
places.description: A cave city that predates the Greek and Roman empires. Tony visited the city with Asia Argento. It used to be where the outcasts were sent, until Hollywood came to make movies. Now there are tourists and AirBnBs.

places.name: Mount View High School
places.description: Tony visited this high school where football is life. Tony attends the pre-game meal of ribs, baked potatoes, and chicken.

places.name: The Coffee Shop
places.description: Tony had biscuits with gravy and fried eggs with journalist Nick Mullins and documentarian Elaine McMillion Sheldon.

places.name: Brooksrun Mining Co
places.description: Tony ate bear meat in a coal mine with miners.

places.name: King Knob Motorsports Park
places.description: Tony visited this off road park of mud and machines (ATVs, motorbikes). On the menu: "frog legs, snapping-turtle patties, catfish (barbecued and fried), smoked bass."

places.name: Keith's Bar & Grille
places.description: Tony had a beer with a Trump supporter to try to understand why they voted for him.

places.name: Lost Creek Farm
places.description: Tony had a meal with Mike Costello and Amy Dawson, chefs and owners of Lost Creek Farm.They had desperation pie,  pawpaw ice cream with candied wildflowers, buttermilk-poached trout with fresh-picked rhubarb, sweet-corn chowder, buttermilk-fried rabbit with fresh maple syrup, venison with a chicory-root rub, chanterelle mushrooms, and wild pears.

places.name: Bar Arocena
places.description: Unofficial sandwich of Uruguay: the Chivito: a steak, ham, bacon, cheese, hard boiled egg, mayo, and garnishes.

places.name: Jacinto
places.description: Tony had dinner with chef and owner Lucía Soria and Nacho. They had "homemade gnocchi with mushrooms and pumpkin; prawns with avocado, apple, and wasabi; beef tartare with quail egg."

places.name: Escaramuza Libros
places.description: Tony had lunch with journalist Carina Novarese. They had homemade sausage with roasted turnip and fermented vegetables, beet quiche.

places.name: Bar Las Flores
places.description: Tony had pizza with the Hablan por la Espalda bandmates. They had pizza a caballo, or “a bizarro stoner hybrid topped with thin chickpea flatbread”

places.name: Cantina del Vigía
places.description: Tony and friends ate a feast of empanadas with corn and shredded, slow-cooked beef; goat cheese and spinach croquettes; fried fish; provoleta (bubbling cheese that “puffs up and inflates on the plate”); whole roasted suckling wild boar; whole roasted fish; beef milanesa covered in tomato sauce, ham, and shredded cheese.

places.name: Las Nenas Steak House
places.description: Tony, Nacho, and Diego went to this drive through to have skirt steak and ribeye, french fries, blood sausages, and beer..

places.name: Parador La Huella
places.description: Beach restaurant where Tony and his brother ate fire roasted prawns and sauteed octopus

places.name: Chafe's Landing Restaurant
places.description: Tony had fish & chips with Dale Jarvis, Intangible Cultural Heritage Development Officer for the Heritage Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador.

places.name: Red Indian Lake
places.description: Tony went with a group of chefs to a place up near the lake to eat some hearty cod stew, various terrines (duck, rabbit, guinea hen, boar), jellied foie gras, fire-roasted moose shoulder and leg, ribs braised with red wine, local boletus, and chanterelle mushrooms.

places.name: Raymonds Restaurant
places.description: Tony, Fred Morin, and Dave McMillan had a seafood tower of "razor clams, snow crab, mussels, capelin, whelks, and sea urchin," boquerones; scallop crudo with smoked roe; tuna prepared with berries; heart of caribou tartare with alder and chanterelle mushrooms; charred whelk with crispy cod sounds (fried fish bladders); cod filet with cod liver canelé, partridge profiteroles, Jiggs dinner (a boiled dish of meat and root veggies) reinvented as a pasta dish, chanterelle ice cream.

places.name: Saint Pierre and Miquelon
places.description: Tony, Fred, and Dave went to this island to visit Maïté Legasse, home cook and director of Ma P’tite Cocotte. They had sea urchin pâté, stuffed squid over rice, braised halibut, local cheese, tart with local baked apples and blackberries.

places.name: The Big R Restaurant
places.description: Tony and a group of chefs had fried bologna which Tony called "awesome," fried clam strips, liver and onions, pan-fried cod tongues, Jiggs dinner, french fries.

places.name: Jack Axes
places.description: Tony, Fred, and Dave threw axes and had a cheese plate, an assortment of patés, and pickled pork tongue.

places.name: Christian's Bar
places.description: Tony met with chefs Fred Morin and Dave McMillan. They had a fried bologna cube and Newfoundland‘s very own Screech Rum.

places.name: Dolmama - Armenia's Restaurant
places.description: "This is what my soul needed,” Tony said as he ate braised lamb shank with rice pilaf; vegetable manti

places.name: Gum Market
places.description: Seasonal produce fresh food market. Tony's hosts shop for ingredients to make a dish called ghapama.

places.name: At Gayane's
places.description: Tony had apricot vodka, and trout wrapped and baked in lavash

places.name: Dargett Craft Beer
places.description: Tony had beers here with Tech journalist Artur Papayan, developer Harut Martirosyan, and cybersecurity consultant Ruben Muradyan.

places.name: Nagorno-Karabakh
places.description: Tony camped out in the wilderness with Tigran Grigoryan, a war veteran and aspiring politician; Tatul Hakobyan, a journalist; Saro Saryan, an Armenian born in Azerbaijan who fled during the war. They had  jingalov hats, or “lavash bread stuffed with greens and onions before being toasted atop a cast-iron sheet” and Armenian barbecue covered in fennel and fresh herbs

places.name: Restaurant Poloz Mukuch
places.description: Tony had a meal with some locals: oxtail soup; stewed liver and heart with tomato, onions, and fresh herbs; sheep’s head.

places.name: Leaf Dessert
places.description: A 100-year old noodle shop. Tony had beef brisket noodles with wontons, cinematographer Chris Doyle had choy noodle soup.

places.name: Keung Kee Dai Pai Dong
places.description: "Cheap, delicious food served from open air stalls." Tony met with chef Gazza Cheng and had drunken chicken, fish tripe, and egg-custard clay-pot dish with youtiao

places.name: Fishing Village Gourmet Food
places.description: Christopher Doyle and filmmaker Jenny Suen accompanied Tony to eat corn–and–fish ball soup; fried fish ball with dried scallop, dried shrimp, and pork; sun cured salted salted egg yolks served over rice; crispy pork belly cooked in shrimp paste and yu choy

places.name: China Cafe
places.description: Tony and photojournalist Simon Go ordered Fried pork cutlet over rice with tomato sauce, soup with macaroni, spam and egg, French toast with peanut butter and condensed milk, pineapple bun.

places.name: Sher-E-Punjab
places.description: Tony had northern Indian food here.

places.name: Sun Hing Restaurant
places.description: Tony went for dim sum with Janice Lau and Jason Cheung, members of Hong Kong punk band David Boring.

places.name: Happy Paradise
places.description: Tony had dinner with Jenny Suen, feng shui master Thierry Chow, and chef May Chow. They had sourdough chicken-fat egg waffle with Taiwanese bottarga; sauteed prawns with pan-roasted pumpkin, dried shrimp roe, and prawn oil; tea-smoked pigeon; Hakka-style chicken; and pig brain with burnt-pear vinaigrette.

places.name: Lau Sum Kee Noodle
places.description: Tony had lunch with Douglas Young, founder of Goods of Desire. They had prawn-roe noodles.

places.name: Domke Nino Fleischerei
places.description: Tony met with Frank Künster, legendary Berlin bouncer. They had pork schnitzel with brown gravy and boulette (pork meatballs). They discussed the nightclub scene in Berlin.

places.name: Michelberger Hotel
places.description: Tony ate at the restaurant in this hotel with Ellen Allien, famous Berlin DJ. They had sausage made from chicken, pig’s feet, and sage with smoked potatoes and apple; braised beef shoulder served with root vegetables, potatoes, horseradish, and kale.

places.name: Grosz (Closed)
places.description: This address was supposed to be a place called Grosz where Tony ate Weimar fare with Historian Brendan Nash; performance artist Le Pustra; and event manager, producer, and promoter Else Edelstahl. Could not find on map.

places.name: Fleamarket at Mauerpark
places.description: Tony visited this flea market with Anton and his son Wolfgang. They purchased records at one stand.

places.name: Zum Schusterjungen
places.description: Tony and rocker Anton Newcombe had a dinner of brined pork knuckle served alongside sauerkraut and boiled potatoes, schnitzel with sautéed seasonal vegetables and creamed mushrooms. And a large beer.

places.name: Lichtblick-Kino
places.description: Tony met with Billy Wagner, co-owner of Nobelhart & Schmutzig at this movie house for currywurst and fries, doner kebab, sparkling wine, and red wine.

places.name: Bellman Bar (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Bellman Bar? Tony met with  film director and photographer Miron Zownir for cocktails here.

places.name: Billy's Boudin and Cracklin
places.description: Tony had a boudin ball (a type of local sausage) stuffed with pepper jack cheese, cracklings, and Budweiser beer with chef Toby Rodriguez

places.name: Laura's Two
places.description: Tony had rice and gravy, fried fish, ribs, and smothered stuffed turkey wings with journalist Herman Fuselier, Creole cowboy Dave Lemelle, musician and business owner Sid Williams.

places.name: Suire's Grocery & Restaurant
places.description: Tony had an oyster po’boy, crawfish étouffée, pecan pie, and beer.

places.name: Menzu Shiri (Closed?)
places.description: Tony and Darren Aronofsky ate momos (dumplings) here. Could not find the restaurant on a map.

places.name: Folk heritage museum restaurant
places.description: Tony tried yak hide sautéed with garlic, tomato, and peppercorns with Darren Aronofsky and environmentalist Dasho Benji Dorji

places.name: Phallus Centre Shop
places.description: Tony and Darren visited this place about an hour outside of the city. They visited a town in this area that specialzes in Phallus Handicraft and bought a few phallic art pieces (wooden penis)

places.name: Pelela Pass
places.description: "The gateway to Central Bhutan," Tony describes. He met with Dr. Nawang Norbu and Darren Aronofsky to have yak butter tea, yak jerky with spiced greens, yak cheese, dried chilies, ara infused with Cordyceps, a fungus that kills its caterpillar-larva host.

places.name: Trongsa
places.description: Tony and Darren had yak butter tea. Tony tried braised pork, pork-bone soup, pepper soup, and rice. Darren tried betelnut which he said made him feel high.

places.name: Membartsho
places.description: Also known as the "Burning Lake" although it is on a river, not a lake. There are clay offerings and incense burning.

places.name: Unknown small restaurants
places.description: Tony met up with W. Kamau Bell in the capital city. They had a lunch of grilled tilapia, ugali, Tusker beer. Tony also had beers with Njeri Gikera, owner of ChilliMango clothing store; Melissa Mbugua, managing partner at MNM Consulting Africa; and Bell at another local place.

places.name: Toi Main Market
places.description: Tony and Kamau went shoe shopping at this discount market

places.name: Kibera slum
places.description: Tony and Kamau visited this area and met with comedian Mammito Eunice, musician Simon Okuku, and comedian Geoffrey Ochieng, members of Kibera Creative Arts.

places.name: Nyeri Shuttle Matatu Stage
places.description: Tony and Kamau rode on the Mad Max bus, which is basically a party on a bus. Afterward they ate goat's head soup and drank beer.

places.name: Lewa Wildlife Conservancy
places.description: Tony and Kamau visited this conservancy to view the animals. They saw a giraffe, termite mounds, zebras, an ostrich, an elephant, a rhino, and some lions. They then had a breakfast of scrambled eggs and toast with Mike Watson, CEO of the conservancy; Faith Riunga, education officer of the conservancy; Wanjiku Kinuthia, communications officer of the conservancy; Tom Lalampaa, CEO of Northern Rangelands Trust

places.name: Il Ngwesi Community
places.description: Tony and Kamau met with Masai chief Jonathan Kip and Masai elder Lela Kinyaga to have a meal in a Masai village. They had cow’s blood mixed with milk, grilled meat, and rice.

places.name: Ca'Laura
places.description: Tony and Chef José Andrés at this plaza café. They had arvejas (green peas) with ham, croquette, mussels, steak topped with blue cheese

places.name: Bar Guillermina
places.description: Tony and José Andrés had a bowl of beans with sausage and some cider. After they ate, they played a game similar to bowling.

places.name: Casa Lin
places.description: Tony and José Andrés had cider, sea urchin, spider crab, percebes (gooseneck barnacles)

places.name: Sobrecueva
places.description: Tony and José Andrés had Cabrales (a type of blue cheese) at this cheese farm. José commented that the cheese had penicillin in it and Tony's retort was, "I can feel my syphilis clearing already!"

places.name: Sidrería Asturias
places.description: Tony and José had lunch with the man who caught el campanu (the season’s first caught salmon) in 2014. They had bread, salami, cheese, olives, beer.

places.name: Casa Eladia, Piñera, Rozaes
places.description: Tony had a free-range chicken stew with rice called Pitu de Caleya.

places.name: Restaurante Güeyu Mar
places.description: Tony and José had fresh caught grilled salmon with local moonshine. They laughed as they ate and drank outside at this place.

places.name: Rumah Makan SURYA Masakan Padang
places.description: Tony ate Padang food: fried lungs, intestine goulash, beef rendang.

places.name: Blok M Square
places.description: Tony had street food at this huge market

places.name: Kamandalu Ubud
places.description: Not sure if this is the hotel where Tony sat at the pool and said “So this, too, is Bali, I guess — or it is now. Thank you, Jimmy Buffett, for taking a big dump on the world. Another wagyu slider, my good man. Yes, thank you. By weight, how much human waste is generated by your average person on vacation. Look around you. Do the math. I want a water sample. I’m telling you, the fecal coliform count is going to be interesting. I’m sure there’s a metaphor here. Am I bitter? Am I just old? Do I just have, like, a predisposition of instinctive hatred of young people? Perhaps. Enjoy the moment. The words of Baba Ram Dass: ‘Be here now.’ But I am here now. I’m not actually having sliders. You eat those fucking things.”
