places.description: A chef Tony calls the pioneer that put Melbourne on the map as the food capital of Australia. Local snapper sashimi, lough oysters, yellowfin tuna with caramelized pork belly and watermelon, freshwater lobster.

places.name: Town Hall Kebab
places.description: Tony really loved the kebab he had here

places.name: Rumi Brunswick
places.description: Lebanese food. Lamb brains, softshell prawns. Tony's favorite thing he ate in Melbourne: barbecue quail and lamb ribs. He liked the quail so much he ordered a second plate.

places.name: Dainty Sichuan Hot Pot - CBD
places.description: "Wonderfully sadomasochistic play between pleasure and pain; between the scorching searing bite of the dried red Sichuan pepper and the cooling more floral relief of the Sichuan flower petal"

places.name: Royal Mail Hotel, Dunkeld
places.description: Sustainable restaurant using ingredients grown/raised on site. The chef trained at Mugaritz, a world-class restaurant in the Basques.

places.name: Imperial Hotel
places.description: Restauranteur Con Christopoulos owns this place across from the Parliament. They grilled shrimp and meats.

places.name: Chaps Pit Beef
places.description: Grilled pit beef with tiger sauce (horseradish mayo) served on rye or a kaiser roll.

places.name: Mo's Seafood
places.description: Garlic crab, oysters, and crab cakes. And a crazy blue drink named The Obama

places.name: The Roost
places.description: Lake Trout = not from a lake and not a trout. It's a "trash fish" that is fried but tastes good. Sides of macaroni & cheese and greens. Also cheap

places.name: Polonia Restaurant
places.description: Seasonal soups, housemade sausages, stuffed cabbage, potato dumplings, roast duck with applesauce, latkes stuffed with mushrooms

places.name: Al Ameer
places.description: Lebanese food. Babaganoush, hummus, taboulleh, kibbe, fresh bread

places.name: Ulrich's 1868 Tavern
places.description: local beer, local food, soup, buffalo wings of course!

places.name: Pirate Cat Cafe (Closed)
places.description: Maple bacon latte. Also a radio station where Tony did an interview. Closed down now.

places.name: Tadich Grill
places.description: Martinis and cioppino. Also hangtown fry: oyster, eggs and bacon.

places.name: Zam Zam
places.description: Martinis

places.name: House of Prime Rib
places.description: Steakhouse heaven. And more martinis.

places.name: Tamales Mi Lupitas
places.description: Tony had chicharron, cabeza, and chorizo tacos.

places.name: Sushi Sebo (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Sushi Sebo, a sushi restaurant that shipped a majority of its fish from Japan, closed now

places.name: That's It
places.description: A liquor store you can eat at. They have tortas! Tony had the Cubano which is huge and filled with chorizo, hot dog, ham, egg, cheese, mayo, jalapeno, and avocado.

places.name: R & G Lounge
places.description: Salt & pepper crab

places.name: Incanto (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Incanto, Chris Cosentino's restaurant

places.name: Red's Java House
places.description: Double cheeseburger, chili cheese fries, and a beer

places.name: Moo Sa-Tae Nai Aood
places.description: Food stand serving ginger and pork sausage

places.name: Likhit Kai Yang
places.description: Tony ate isan style chicken and green papaya salad, which he described as so hot, "I think my pubes just caught on fire."

places.name: The Old Saloon
places.description: Tony had beers here

places.name: Pinky's Cafe
places.description: Pasty. Meat and potatoes in a dough and covered in gravy.

places.name: 2nd Street Bistro
places.description: Best chef in Montana, according to Tony. Buffalo, elk, and chicken gallantine, pork rilette. Braised whitefish stuffed with lightly smoked trout with a white butter sauce. Lavender rack of lamb with potatoes. Shortribs with morels. All local ingredients

places.name: Zia Forica
places.description: Octopus, anchovy, cordula, fava beans, snails

places.name: Château Richeux
places.description: Chef Olivier Roellinger's restaurant

places.name: Grain de Vanille
places.description: Chef Olivier Roellinger's bakery specializing with salted butter caramels, and sand cookies

places.name: Le Ty Billig
places.description: Ham, egg, and cheese crepes

places.name: Chez Jacky
places.description: Tony finally got his huge seafood tower!

places.name: Minetta Tavern
places.description: In this episode about food obsessions, Tony met with Josh Ozersky (who died in 2015) to try the La Frieda black label burger. Ozersky was obsessed with beef.

places.name: Botanical Hotel
places.description: Paul Wilson, head chef at the time of this shooting, cooked wagyu beef on a grill

places.name: Sullivan Street Bakery and Pizza
places.description: Jim Lahey, owner and baker. Obsessed with dough/bread. The restaurant on the show, called "Co." (short for Company) closed, but he is still making pizza at Sullivan Street Bakery's Chelsea location.

places.name: Txikito
places.description: Tony ate here with Steve Plotniki who is a food writer, who says this restaurant is like San Sebastian, Spain, but in NYC.

places.name: Kampuchea Restaurant
places.description: Crispy pork belly, tamarind baby back ribs

places.name: Esca
places.description: "The man knows fish," Tony says of the chef.

places.name: Bar du Marché
places.description: At 8 am, the men gather and eat charcuterie, bread, and cheese and drink red wine

places.name: La Galline
places.description: Melon and prosciutto

places.name: The Little Duke
places.description: Tony tried making pastries here

places.name: Mercado de Mariscos
places.description: Tony had extremely fresh ceviche at this fish market

places.name: Kwang Chow Chinese Dive
places.description: Old school Cantonese Panamanian food. Roast duck, wontons.Westernized Chinese food.

places.name: Sorolo
places.description: Local seafood known for their fresh marinated fish, fried. And soup.

places.name: Rumeli Kale Cafe & Restaurant
places.description: Turkish tea, fried cheese, olives, assorted breads

places.name: Çiçek Pasajı
places.description: Outdoor eating area with traditional foods served like tapas.

places.name: Sur Ocakbaşı
places.description: The speciality is devian. The lamb is specially raised as free-range in highlands in the east.

places.name: Pivovar U Medvídků
places.description: Traditional Czech restaurant

places.name: Pivovarský klub
places.description: Beer from regional producers,

places.name: Kavárna V Sedmém Nebi
places.description: Tony had some beers here

places.name: Café Savoy
places.description: Tony met a guide here that promised to take him to eat some pho.

places.name: Pho Quynh Anh
places.description: After lots of delays where the crew could not secure permission to film, they finally got to eat at this Vietnamese restaurant in Little Hanoi.

places.name: Wenceslas Square
places.description: Tony's continuous search for "meat in tube form" leads him to this sausage stand. It's a fast food place, so his chef friend is surprised how much he likes it.

places.name: Hudson River
places.description: They ate hot dogs from Dick's Dogs hot dog boat as they sailed down the river. Then they ate crabs they caught in the river.

places.name: Quaker Creek Store
places.description: Polish traditional foods. Pierogi, Kielbasa

places.name: Rondout Bay Cafe & Marina
places.description: Working man's bar on the marina.

places.name: X2O Xaviars on the Hudson
places.description: Tony ate lunch with Bill Murray here

places.name: Esquina de la Ronda (Closed)
places.description: This restaurant no longer exists, but it was a traditional Ecuadorian restaurant

places.name: Picateria el Hueco
places.description: Tony tries cuy, which is a local specialty made from roasted guinea pig

places.name: Pelicano Restaurante.
places.description: Tony had fresh seafood on the beach

places.name: Market
places.description: Tony had black clams, a regional specialty. It was made into a ceviche with shrimp, red onion, cilantro, tomato, and roasted corn. Sprinkled with fried banana chips

places.name: La Guatita
places.description: Tony ate tripe soup. His guide had a fish stew with plantains (casuela).

places.name: El Pez Volador
places.description: Encebollado, a yellowfin tuna specialty of the region, a fish stew. Tony describes the place as, "A simple food stall in a nondescript corner in Downtown Guayaquil."

places.name: Fulton Fish Market
places.description: These seafood guys made lobster in the warehouse

places.name: Chez Pépin
places.description: Jacques does a demo making an omelet.

places.name: Scarpetta
places.description: Scott Conant demonstrates how to make red sauce and pasta

places.name: The French Laundry
places.description: Thomas Keller demonstrates how to perfectly roast a chicken

places.name: Js Oyster
places.description: Oysters and beer.

places.name: Street & Co
places.description: Seafood restaurant

places.name: Primo Restaurant
places.description: Sustainable, seasonal local and made from scratch meals.

places.name: Fox Pub
places.description: Tony's handler is from Belfast, and owns this pub

places.name: Zhangfei Braised Pork
places.description: Pork centric belly warming foods to warm you up. And beer

places.name: Zhaoji Laopu
places.description: Manchu style dinner - imperial cuisine of the Qing Dynasty

places.name: Donghe Barbecue Yabuli Branch
places.description: Tony had grilled meats on a stick from a mobile food stand

places.name: Dasong Dumplings
places.description: This place is a 24-hour restaurant known for their dumplings

places.name: Around the World
places.description: Classic Russian restaurant. With vodka.

places.name: Shazhutian
places.description: Large bustling restaurant hall serving all kinds of pork preparations

places.name: Hanoi Food Stand
places.description: "The dish I crave above all others," Tony says of pho. This episode was a clip show showing "food porn" examples.

places.name: Bar Boulud
places.description: "Food for the gods" with boudin noir, "one of the very best things on earth"

places.name: Salumeria Rosi
places.description: Cured meats. Tony's go-to salumeria in NYC

places.name: Sushi Yasuda
places.description: "Sushi is first about the rice." Chef Yasuda had been doing it for 32 years at the filming of this episode.

places.name: Porter House Bar and Grill
places.description: Steaks! Tony had the cowboy ribeye.

places.name: The Campbell
places.description: Tony had a Manhattan, Negroni, and a Sidecar made by bartender Dale

places.name: The Rub BBQ (Closed)
places.description: Former site of "The Rub BBQ" where Tony had brisket

places.name: Jacques Torres
places.description: Tony ate chocolate

places.name: DBGB (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Boulud's fresh sausage place, DBGB

places.name: Marea
places.description: Italian gastronomy by Chef Michael White

places.name: Petrossian Boutique & Cafe
places.description: Tony had champagne and caviar

places.name: Happy Island
places.description: A man-made island made of conch shells with a bar and cocktails

places.name: Sun Beach & Eat Seckie & Vanessa
places.description: Ingredients grown in garden or locally, and cocktails

places.name: Tante Pearls Resturant
places.description: Tony was hungover from eating manicou stew (opossum stew) on the beach and drinking strong rum. He ate here the next morning: Caribbean Conch Curry, Souse soup

places.name: Bully's Bar (Closed)
places.description: There is (was?) a bar in this area called Bully's Bar, can't find it on the map anymore. A local bar, people told Tony it was in a "bad part of town" but he went anyway and said everyone was lovely

places.name: Kihachi (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Kihachi, a Japanese restaurant by retired chef Mike Kimura.

places.name: Clever Crows Pizza
places.description: Creative pizzas with housemade sausage

places.name: Perla's
places.description: Seafood spot with 20 different kinds of oysters

places.name: Gourdough's Big. Fat. Donuts.
places.description: Donut truck. Fried chicken donuts, Jalapeno donuts, all kinds of weird topping donuts

places.name: Odd Duck
places.description: Farm to table food truck. Grilled quail, brussel sprouts, pork belly

places.name: East Side King
places.description: "Japanese drunk food fusion" is how Tony described this food truck.

places.name: Mizuna
places.description: Tony had a medley of foie gras prepared many different ways. Chef Bonanno was trained at the French Laundry.

places.name: Biker Jim's Gourmet Dogs
places.description: Street cart serving different sausages and hot dogs

places.name: Bacchus - A Bartolotta Restaurant
places.description: Wisconsin beer cheese soup; crispy egg mollet with curry potato salad, crème fraiche, and caviar; foie gras french toast, bone marrow

places.name: Piccolo (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Piccolo restaurant, closed in 2017

places.name: World Trade Center
places.description: Grilled meats. Tony and the Bishop visited after the church service

places.name: STEEL HOUSE
places.description: Tony ate at a street food stand outside this building. Fish, quail, lentils.

places.name: Toddy Shop
places.description: Toddy is an alcoholic drink, but Tony was not a fan. He was a fan of the food served here. Known for their fish head curry. He also tried steamed pearl spot fish curry (local fish), and crab masala.

places.name: Ravi Restaurant
places.description: Pakistani restaurant with Punjabi food and naan

places.name: Bu Qtair Restaurant مطعم بوقطير‎
places.description: Choose your own fish and shrimp. Catch of the day is served with a spicy rub and coconut curry sauce

places.name: Verre (Closed)
places.description: Formerly Verre, by Gordon Ramsay (closed)

places.name: Cafe' De Paris
places.description: Seasonal restaurant; Tony had a dish that had fresh artichokes, fava, peas, and pancetta. Also a baby lamb dish, a salad with roman chicory and anchovy.

places.name: Trattoria Il Timoniere Roma
places.description: Tony ate carbonara and amatriciana

places.name: Hotel Majestic
places.description: Tony ate at the Sicilian restaurant in the hotel. Caponata, bucatini with sardines. Fish, potatoes, fava, pistachio, tomato.

places.name: Trattoria A Casa Di Rita
places.description: Tony and his wife Ottavia ate here with one of the cameraman. Antipasti; tagliolini with garlic, chiles, and pork ragout; oxtail; tripe.

places.name: Le Chef
places.description: Tony went back to Le Chef, where he ate on his last visit (when Beirut was bombed). They had hummus with pine nuts and ground beef, eggplant with tomatoes, chickpeas, and onions.  Fun story he told on this episode: The airport got bombed so after he finally got home taking a ship back to NYC, his girlfriend Ottavia met him when the boat docked and they conceived their daughter.

places.name: Tawlet
places.description: A rotating cast of cooks provide regional foods from the artisanal market. Labneh, Armenian meat pie, artichoke and fava bean salad, fuul.

places.name: Onno Bistro - Bourj Hammoud
places.description: Tony ate Kibbeh, phyllo, Armenian cheese, spicy potatoes, meat & cheese filled pastries, hummus, stuffed grape leaves

places.name: Frank Würst Fine Hotdog
places.description: They had drunken BBQ and chili dogs.

places.name: Falafel M. Sahyoun
places.description: Two falafel shops owned by brothers who do not speak to each other, right next door to each other.

places.name: Falafel M Sayhoun Original Shop
places.description: Two falafel shops owned by brothers who do not speak to each other, right next door to each other.

places.name: Massaya Winery
places.description: Anise seed liquor, red and white table wines. Food at the restaurant changes seasonally. Tony ate potato salad with parsley, olive oil, and nuts; Lebanese spaghetti (served with yogurt); cabbage salad; grilled eggplant, tomato, and haloumi, sheep's milk cheese, labneh.

places.name: La Tete Dans les Olives
places.description: Tony had lunch with Eric Ripert and 2 Parisian chefs

places.name: Les Cocottes
places.description: Casual, friendly Parisian cuisine

places.name: Frenchie
places.description: One menu, prix fixe, the chef is the only one who cooks. The chef trained at Gramercy Tavern in NYC

places.name: Le Jeu de Quilles (closed)
places.description: Tony had breakfast here with Eric Ripert

places.name: Le Papillon
places.description: Tony, Eric Ripert, and Joel Robouchon ate oysters at this cafe

places.name: Le Châteaubriand
places.description: A single prix fixe menu every day

places.name: L’Avant Comptoir de la Terre
places.description: Tony stopped by this small bar next to the restaurant and had small plates

places.name: Le comptoir
places.description: Amuse bouche of foie gras; Crab with flying fish eggs and spring vegetables in a foie gras gelee; Blood sausage ravioli (one the best things Tony had all year); Roasted veal with sauteed chanterelles with an almond rhubarb jus; cheese platter. They do a bistro menu during the day with the best ouef mayonnaise (deviled eggs) on earth.

places.name: L'Atelier de Joël Robuchon
places.description: Tony ate here with Joel supervising the meal. Gazpacho; sole meuniere; Robuchon mashed potatoes

places.name: Restaurante Casa Salvador
places.description: Spanish style restaurant, food like what Spaniards eat at home. Braised oxtail is the specialty

places.name: San Mamés
places.description: Famous for the Madrid Callos (tripe)

places.name: DiverXO
places.description: Spanish seafood with Chinese influence

places.name: La Gabinoteca
places.description: Modern tapas. Foie gras with a sweet crispy pineapple topping; homemade veal sausage topped with housemade ketchup, cheese sauce, and crispy fried onions; gabas al ajillo (shrimp pounded thin and served with roe); chicken wings marinated in lime, honey, soy

places.name: El Bohío
places.description: Castillian food. Diego and Pepe (brothers) run this restaurant. modern twists on traditional dishes using hyper-local ingredients

places.name: El Aljibe
places.description: Specialty is roast chicken in orange sauce with rice and black beans

places.name: Paladar Los Amigos
places.description: Small restaurant. Tony had Masa de cerdo (pork) and pork cutlet served with yucca, rice, and beans

places.name: El Templete
places.description: Basque chef from Spain. Marmitako (seafood stew)

places.name: The 8 Restaurant (Grand Lisboa)
places.description: Michelin starred dim sum restaurant. Roasted chicken with honey lime sauce; dumplings in the shape of goldfish; Roasted pork belly; fish ball soup; crab claw steamed, served with ginger, white wine, egg white

places.name: Macau Military Club Restaurant
places.description: Tony had a gin & tonic with a priest

places.name: Apomac
places.description: Specialize in Macanese food.

places.name: Fernando's Restaurant
places.description: Portuguese Restaurant serving grilled chorizo; clams with garlic, white wine, cilantro, malagueta peppers; grilled sardines; suckling pig

places.name: Leiteria I Son
places.description: Chinese dish called Steamed Milk Pudding

places.name: Tai Lei Loi Kei
places.description: Pork chop bun, a Macanese specialty. It's a pork chop on a fluffy bun.

places.name: Pizzeria Pellone
places.description: Margherita pizza

places.name: Pappacarbone
places.description: Rocco made lunch for Tony at his restaurant. A dish with 2 types of clams and pasta; octopus in a red sauce

places.name: Al Convento
places.description: A convent converted into a restaurant, only ingredients that are fresh and currently in season are served

places.name: Torrisi
places.description: Making Italian recipes from local NY ingredients

places.name: El Bulli (Closed)
places.description: El Bulli was once located here, closed in 2011. The famous restaurant of the Adria brothers. Jose Andres (who trained at El Bulli) met up with Tony to eat there.

places.name: Tickets
places.description: La vida tapa. Albert Adria is the chef. "Whimsical, theatrical; a carnival fairway-like feeling to its dining room," says Tony

places.name: Pappy & Harriet's
places.description: Tony met up with Queens of the Stone Age band member Josh Homme. They serve BBQ. Josh had the rib eye and Tony had the ribs

places.name: Lord Fletcher's Restaurant
places.description: Tony and Josh from Queens of the Stone Age had Yorkshire Pudding with creamed spinach. This restaurant has been around since 1966

places.name: Restoran "Balaklava"
places.description: Tony called this restaurant Tatchaninmis. Tony and Zamir drank several bottles of vodka and ate seafood here.

places.name: Le Borsch (Closed)
places.description: Tony and Zamir went to a restaurant on this street called Le Borsch. It is closed now,  the place had 5 kinds of Borscht and Tony had the green borscht.

places.name: Kyivska perepichka
places.description: Tony got his "meat in tube form" from this place, which deep fries the dough with the meat inside. "It's like a hot dog donut," Tony said.

places.name: McFoxy
places.description: Fast food that looks very similar to McDonalds except with items called Foxy Balls and Foxy Burger. They got Foxy Nuggets, Foxy Balls, Foxy Fries, and a Chicken bacon sandwich with an "assertive mustard sauce," Tony described.

places.name: Abu Shahab City Restaurant
places.description: Tony ate with soldiers from the US Army who were stationed there. Tony had kebab.

places.name: Ogden Museum of Southern Art
places.description: Miss Linda's Yakamein food truck is parked here sometimes. Tony ate this concoction of noodles, beef, egg, and scallions from her truck.

places.name: Willie Mae's Scotch House
places.description: Red beans & rice, and the famous fried chicken

places.name: Glenda's Creole Kitchen Restaurant
places.description: Popular local spot serving Cajun foods. Crab & shrimp with okra, stuffed turkey wings, catfish.

places.name: DBC Bar & Grill
places.description: On his way to the airport, Tony stopped here for a "house special" which includes jungle juice, island citrus, 190 proof vodka and rum.

places.name: Nor Joe Import Company
places.description: Tony also stopped here on the way to the airport to pick up a muffuletta sandwich.

places.name: Barney Greengrass
places.description: Tony had a platter delivered with Nova Scotia salmon, whitefish salad, smoked sturgeon, capers, and assorted fixings.

places.name: El Quinto Pino
places.description: A tiny restaurant specializing in Catalonian food. He ate with the chefs, including: escudella (a boiled dish made during the holidays with beef shank, poultry, turnips, carrots, chickpeas, pig ear, blood sausage, cornbread, cabbage, meatballs called pelota), as well as a brothy soup with galettes with shell pasta, and a canalon (leftover meat from escudella cooked into a new dish with pasta and cheese)

places.name: Blaue Gans (Closed)
places.description: Former site of Blaue Gans, an Austrian restaurant that closed in 2018.

places.name: The Spotted Pig
places.description: Chef April Broomfield met with Tony at her house (not at this location) to cook a proper English holiday meal. They cooked a duck and some savory pie with veal shank, pig trotter, garlic, chanterelle mushrooms, swiss chard.

places.name: Avenue Maïs Gaté
places.description: This area on the edge of Port-au-Prince has a market with a lot of street food available. Laloo (a leafy green veggie with bits of crab, okra, and peppers), rice & beans, pork & chicken in Creole sauce with picklese (a spicy slaw).

places.name: Assiette Creole
places.description: In the relatively affluent area of Haiti, which was less damaged by the earthquake, Tony ate Creole food with some locals.

places.name: Krong Kampot
places.description: Somewhere between Angkor Wat and this port city, Tony stopped at a roadside restaurant which served freshly made spring rolls and noodles.

places.name: Ta Eou Restaurant
places.description: Tony had lunch with Mu Sochua, an ethnic Cambodian-Chinese member of Parliament.

places.name: Oudongk
places.description: Tony ate noodles for breakfast at a roadside stand in this town. He then took a lorry (a makeshift traincar made of stacked wood) and stopped at the Soys, a family on a rice farm living in a traditional stilt house. They cooked him chicken with shredded banana flowers, leben, and coriander. Sour beef stew with lemongrass, turmeric, fermented fish, and kantrop leaves. Sticky rice cooked in bamboo, and a dessert made from palm sugar.

places.name: Special Happy Pizza
places.description: Tony had a "happy pizza" which included "powerful herbs"

places.name: Phnom Penh Night Market
places.description: Tony had another bowl of noodle soup here

places.name: Managua
places.description: After their shoot was canceled when authorities did not want them filming street racing, Tony and his driver and friends went to a hamburger stand on the street for burgers and hot dogs.

places.name: Terminal de Autobuses Mercado Roberto Huembes
places.description: Market, depot, and food court. Tony and his friend Hector ate a platter of beef & onions with rice

places.name: Vaho tradicional
places.description: Cassava and plantain cooked slowly in banana leaves with marinated beef with garlic, onions, tomato, and bitter orange, layered with green onions and red peppers.

places.name: Flor De Pino
places.description: Run by a woman who has cooked for nearly every important political figure. No longer open to the public. Some dishes include: Carne de Monte (iguana) and bull nuts.

places.name: Leche agria El Ganadero
places.description: Tony called this place El Vaquero. A soured milk yogurt drink is supposed to be a hangover cure. Nacatamales is corn masa inside plantain leaves filled with pork, tomato, and herbs. Tony said it was the best meal he ate so far in the trip.

places.name: Weingut & Heuriger Zahel
places.description: A restaurant with a vineyard attached. Blood sausage, head cheese, bread with schmaltz and onion. Goose liver and onions. Pheasant breast wrapped in speck (proscuitto) and dumplings.

places.name: Gut Purbach
places.description: In the wine country, the chef raises his own sheep to serve at the restaurant

places.name: Schönbrunn Palace
places.description: Tony went to the Kris Kringle market in front of the palace. He had a spezialpunsch and goulash from a stand called Wiener Spiralkartoffel

places.name: Café Sacher Wien
places.description: "Sachertorte, a staple for the Viennese coffee and pastry culture."

places.name: Gastwirtschaft Wratschko
places.description: Traditional Austrian food. Meatballs with gravy and potatoes; tafelspitz (Austrian version of pot au feu with boiled beef shoulder); pan roasted goose breast with quince and red cabbage; beuschel mit serviettenknoedel (cream stew of veal hearts, lungs, liver, and dumplings.

places.name: Riesen-Hot-Dog Bratwürstel
places.description: In search for a cheese-filled sausage (kasekreiner), Tony and friends stopped at this hot dog stand

places.name: Rumors Cocktail Lounge
places.description: Arm wrestling tournament. Hunting gear is appropriate wear for this bar.

places.name: Fred & Red's
places.description: Tony tried the legendary Spaghetti Red., a plate of spaghetti with pickles, onions, and parmesan.

places.name: Belle Isle Seafood
places.description: Clam chowder, fried clams, fried shrimp, lobster, onion rings, fried haddock, etc. Lobster rolls.

places.name: Michael's Deli
places.description: Tony tries the Howie Carr, named after the radio station host. It is a huge sandwich with corned beef and beef brisket. And pastrami knishes.

places.name: Galley Diner
places.description: Corned beef hash.with runny eggs and potatoes. HUGE omelets

places.name: Eire Pub
places.description: They had Guinness and beef barley soup

places.name: Rondo's Submarine Sandwiches
places.description: For a big fat greasy sandwich, "a temple of big sandwich arts" which Tony also describes as "happiness"

places.name: O Senhor Ramos (Closed)
places.description: This used to be the location of O Senhor Ramos, a Portuguese restaurant. Now closed.

places.name: Murphys Law
places.description: Tony had drinks here and played darts

places.name: L Street Tavern
places.description: They had beer and complimentary beef stew

places.name: Quencher Tavern
places.description: A tiny bar where Tony had more drinks and did shots

places.name: Aji No Karyu
places.description: Tony had a very untraditional miso ramen that included butter and corn.

places.name: Wakakoma Restaurant
places.description: Tony craved sea urchin and salmon roe.

places.name: Yakitori Torimatsu
places.description: The master Akihiro Matsuosa grills meats

places.name: Rakuichi Soba
places.description: The chef, Master Tatsui Rai, makes the noodles by hand.

places.name: Copacabana
places.description: Tony had piri piri chicken here. It is a spicy grilled chicken basted with piri piri chili sauce (piri piri peppers, lime, peanut oil, minced garlic, tomato).

places.name: Joe's Kansas City Bar-B-Que
places.description: Tony ate here with Zamir and a food blogger. The blogger says this is the best place to get BBQ in the city. It was called Oklahoma Joe's when they attended. Tony loves the ribs and burnt ends.

places.name: Jack Stack Barbecue | Freight House
places.description: Zamir and Tony ordered bourbon, short ribs, lamb ribs, spare ribs.

places.name: Woodyard Bar-B-Que
places.description: Tony had BBQ here with The Black Keys

places.name: B.B.'s Lawnside BBQ

places.description: Bringing together different regions of artisanal and organic foods in a market. "Southern coastal bread bakers, kibbeh specialists from the mountains, wild herbs & vegetables from the Bekaa Valley, and pastries made from almost forgotten recipes. It's all here," Tony says.

places.name: Vadorin Maurice
places.description: Meat shop selling offal.

places.name: Hugo Desnoyer
places.description: Butcher shop with charcuterie

places.name: Rue Mouffetard
places.description: Open air market. Tony went there with Eric Ripert and Joel Robuchon

places.name: Mercado de San Miguel
places.description: A century old market with fresh produce, seafood, locally made goods

places.name: Avenida de Bélgica
places.description: Mercado Egido, a fresh produce market

places.name: Bessarabsky Market
places.description: 100 year old market selling fruits, veggies, meat, fish. Tony and Zamir shopped there for a picnic. Tony bought the good caviar, which he implies is sturgeon caviar from Caspian Sea which is illegal.

places.name: Dali Shijiagong
places.description: Street market every month.

places.name: Qardaran Tea Shop
places.description: The market near the citadel is "a dizzying maze of alleys and vendors" where you can buy everything from guns to food. Tony had tea at this shop

places.name: Central Market
places.description: Tony was upset that this market he went to 10 years ago was now more commercialized. He went to a smaller local and less touristy market and had Ka Tiew, a noodle soup (they did not say where this was)

places.name: Crab Market
places.description: Tony met Madame Kek, a humanitarian, in this seaside town that used to be a resort town for rich people before 1970 and is now in ruins. They went to the crab market and bought crabs, shrimp, rice, and rambutan.

places.name: Naschmarkt
places.description: At Naschmarkt, they stopped at this butcher with a small kitchen in the back room to make cordon bleu. Tony and host bought the ingredients in the market

places.name: Urbanek
places.description: Cured meats and cheeses in a food market called the Naschmarkt.

places.name: Ver-o-Peso Market
places.description: Tony tastes many fruits here not found anywhere else. He also has fish called Filhote, a 600 lb. freshwater fish.

places.name: Belém
places.description: A market on the river has fruits, vegetables, and fish found in this area

places.name: Captain Gourmet
places.description: Tony picked up food supplies here for a boat trip

places.name: CENTRAL WHOLESALE SEAFOOD MARKET
places.description: Huge seafood market with crab, fish, and more.

places.name: Mercado de Peixe
places.description: They bought seafood here, then brought it to Esplanada Belinha to have it cooked. Clams steamed with garlic; prawns grilled and basted with butter with nshima (thick porridge made of ground maize).

places.name: Market Alvalade Norte
places.description: Large fish market where Tony and Chef Henrique Sa Pessoa visited. They also have produce, cheese, and meat.

places.name: TSUKIJI FISH MARKET
places.description: Fresh fish market

places.name: New Lane Street Foodstalls
places.description: "Street by day, food wonderland by night." Tony had Char Koay Teow, a fried rice stick with bean sprouts, chili, and prawns.

places.name: Red Garden Food Paradise
places.description: A food court full of stands. Tony and guide had fish head curry.

places.name: Boucherie Charcuterie Volailles

places.name: Victor Churchill
places.description: "One of the most beautiful butcher shops in the world"

places.name: Formaggeria salumeria gastronomia Cavallotti
places.description: Local cheese shop. Tony's chef friend made uvo raviolo.

places.name: Quadrilatero
places.description: Tony met with Chef Michael White in this food market

places.name: Tre Monti Azienda Agricola srl
places.description: Vineyard. The chef in the vineyard's kitchen made tagliatelle. They ate it outside with a Sangiovese made at the vineyard.

places.name: Rocinha
places.description: Tony visited this favela where he was tricked into trying a "natural Viagra" by his guide.

places.name: Municipal Market RJ - CADEG
places.description: Many stands were closed when he visited this food market

places.name: Náttúrufræðistofnun Íslands

places.description: Spa

places.name: Aldo Coppola Жуковка
places.description: Spa treatments

places.name: BBQ Box
places.description: This was the location of Master Soon Reflexology where Tony was treated. Not there anymore.

places.name: Garut Regency
places.description: Watched ram fights. Visited the snack bar and had some nasi goreng (Indonesian fried rice)

places.name: Madame Marie
places.description: Fortune Teller

places.name: Sewers Museum

places.name: Anogia, Rethymno

places.name: Corso Calatafimi, 922
places.description: Cuci Lampo - tailor

places.description: Tony plays hockey

places.description: Tony skeptically went to an ashram named Sunil.

places.name: Jaisalmer
places.description: Tony took a bus through the desert out to Jaisalmer, a walled city known as The Golden City. Was a major trade route point in the past

places.name: K-타이거즈
places.description: Tae Kwon Do school

places.name: Le Royal Hotel Beirut
places.description: Tony cooked in the hotel restaurant. The airport was on fire and the there were bombs going off everywhere, but he felt relieved to be able to cook

places.name: Black Taxi Tour Belfast
places.description: Tony went on a tour including the Oliver Crowell mural

places.name: Sundays Well Boating & Tennis Club
places.description: Rugby

places.name: Gubbeen Farm House Products Limited
places.description: Farm with dairy, meat, produce

places.name: Velveteria: The Museum of Velvet Art
places.description: Very strange velvet art; Tony wanted to buy the Mr. T portrait

places.name: Himalayas
places.description: Tony had yak milk tea and yak at someone's house

places.name: Westside Rifle & Pistol Range
places.description: Tony shot rifles

places.name: Edgewater Pier
places.description: Not sure if this is the park where they surfed, but close enough. And it was cold.

places.name: Willard Park
places.description: They went to see the "Free Stamp" sculpture

places.name: Zubal Books
places.description: A very special bookstore with endless shelves of books. Tony visited with Harvey Pekar, comic book writer of American Splendor fame

places.name: Hong Kong Jockey Club Happy Valley Racecourse
places.description: Tony lost money betting on horses here

places.name: New Territories
places.description: Jackie Chan's stunt team helped Tony learn some martial arts stunts. Then they ate Poon Choi: a large bowl layered with vegetables, prawns, fish, mushrooms, pork, and chicken. A thousand year old dish

places.name: Estancia Los Corrales
places.description: Cow ranch

places.name: Marina di Pisa
places.description: Tony's friend Nico invited him to eat at his family home in Marina di Pisa. They cooked a bunch of fresh seafood.

places.name: Restaurant Pizzeria Zum Tor
places.description: Not sure if this is the same Zum Tor. On the show it was a "gentlemen's club" where men gather, drink and, play games. They played Okey, a Turkish game with numbered tiles

places.name: CHAMÄLEON Theater GmbH
places.description: Cabaret dancing

places.name: Derby Dolls
places.description: Roller derby

places.name: Electronic Arts Canada
places.description: Tony was made into a video game character

places.name: Vancouver Film Studios
places.description: Tony filmed a short skit in this film studio

places.name: Banzai Pipeline
places.description: Tony rode around on a jet ski here, the best surfing place in the world

places.name: Lavalleja Department
places.description: Tony and his brother head into the interior, the country. Met the Anrich family, who live on a ranch called Estancia los Morteros. They eat many things including an armadillo caught by the son

places.name: Punta del Este
places.description: Tony spent time on the beach while his brother did sightseeing

places.name: Shields Date Garden
places.description: Date farm with café, gift shop, and gardens. Tony had a date milkshake

places.name: Rio Chama
places.description: Tony went whitewater rafting on the Chama River

places.name: Cavender's Boot City
places.description: Tony bought cowboy boots here

places.description: Kendo master Tadao Todo did a kendo demonstration for Tony

places.name: Rokkokuda Temple
places.description: The birthplace of Ikebana (the art of flower arranging). Tony learned how to make a flower arrangement

places.description: Not sure if this is the correct address. Morimoto buys his knives from Inotada Knife Productions in Sakai City.

places.name: El Bullilab
places.description: Tony visited the El Bulli lab where Adrian tests out ideas for the restaurant

places.name: Enric Rovira Chocolates
places.description: Chocolate factory of a famous chocolatier who is inspired by Gaudi

places.name: International Spy Museum
places.description: Tony talked with a retired spy and visited the museum

places.name: D C Central Kitchen Inc
places.description: Training facility that gives second chances to convicted felons out of prison. They take leftover ingredients from restaurants and hotels and make food for the needy

places.name: Latin American Gym
places.description: Wrestling

places.name: Sant'Erasmo
places.description: Some of the best fruits and vegetables are grown here due to the great soil. Tony raved about a tomato, "It's nice to be reminded of how good a tomato can be" and "a near religious experience." They also drank Fragolina and sliced salami, good cheese, and crusty bread.

places.name: Azores
places.description: Tony's guide Carlos lives in the Azores and his wife makes a Portuguese feast called Terresmos de vinha de alhos, "basically a panorama of delicious pork products, blood sausage, boiled sweet potatoes, and red peppers

places.name: Furnas
places.description: Tony was not a fan of the hot spring he said smelled "like a wet fart"

places.name: Pico Island
places.description: Tony visited a home on this island. Octopus and octopus stew, pico wine, moray eel, locally raised potatoes, local tuna cooked on an open fire.

places.name: São Jorge
places.description: Best cockles on earth, but located in a VERY isolated area that is VERY hard to get to.

places.name: Tom Tom Tamale
places.description: Tamale on a hot dog covered in chili

places.name: Rifka Catering Company
places.description: They had biryani

places.name: Sinigama
places.description: Tony visited this area that had been devastated from a tsunami

places.name: Foundation of Goodness MCC Centre of Excellence
places.description: A man named Kushil opened up his home after the flooding from the tsunami and this evolved into an entire network of services including a medical center, a preschool, a business & services center, and recreational facilities free for everyone to use. They also feed Tony red rice, yellow lentil curry, claypot simmered tuna, jackfruit curry, and coconut sambal during the visit

places.name: Chilaw
places.description: Tony visited a home here where he was served many authentic Sri Lankan dishes, including the spiciest dish in Sri Lanka, crab curry

places.name: Buddhist temple
places.description: Tony paid respects to Mom Gow by visiting her ashes in this Buddhist temple

places.name: Viña Casa Silva
places.description: Winery featuring Carmenère wine (local). There was a rodeo on site and dinner of beef, pork, and chicken.

places.name: Trugo
places.description: Some kind of weird game with a donut puck and a mallet like croquet

places.name: Cadieux Cafe
places.description: Feather bowling: a Belgian feather bowling place with mussels and fries, and live music

places.name: Dan Bailey's Fly Shop
places.description: Tony bought some fly fishing gear

places.name: Del Maguey Single Village Mezcal
places.description: Ron Cooper makes mezcal here

places.name: The Culinary Institute of America
places.description: Tony returned to his alma mater with Mark Ruhlman

places.name: Milo
places.description: Tony stayed in this little town with his cameraman for the show. They did woodsman things like chop down trees and eat venison , rabbit, and bacon

places.name: Yabuli Ski Resort
places.description: Tony went to this resort just as it opened, so there were very few people around. He did some skiing on the icy mountain.

places.name: Schoolcraft College
places.description: Tony visited Chef Brian Polcyn here where he teaches charcuterie and other cooking classes

places.name: Carey Street
places.description: Somewhere on this street is the Alpha Haitai Social & Athletic Club. Chinese gunpowder tea served here, where intellectuals meet and play scrabble!

places.name: 47 Gurley St
places.description: World Resurrection Ministries, where Tony attended a church service

places.name: Robertsport
places.description: Tony had food poisoning when he went to the beach here for surfing

places.name: Mall of the Emirates
places.description: Tony went SKIING in this mall. In the middle of the desert.

places.name: Estadio Latinoamericano
places.description: Home of the Industriales. It was a dream of Tony's to watch a baseball game in Cuba.

places.name: Grand Lisboa hotel
places.description: Tony won $3,000 at the Baccarat tables in the casino

places.name: Coloane Karting Track
places.description: Kart racing

places.name: Macau Tower Convention and Entertainment Center
Porto di Cetara
places.description: Tony watched as a diver/chef  jumped off the cliff and harvested sea urchin. They ate it fresh out of the ocean.

places.name: Sky Village Outdoor Marketplace
places.description: Tony and Josh from Queens of the Stone Age went to this swap meet. They bought some things and ate at the Sky Cafe.

places.name: Rancho de la Luna
places.description: Tony visited the studio where Josh from Queens of the Stone Age made some songs for the show. Tony cooked some food: asparagus risotto with black truffle and pecorino. At the end of the episode, they return to the studio where Tony makes spaghetti pomodoro; Dave makes jalapeno cheese cornbread and collard greens; B.O.C. makes veggie wraps with a soy peanut dipping sauce; Josh makes a red cabbage salad with vinegar, cracked red pepper, and crumbled Roquefort dressing; Hutch makes pulled pork.

places.name: Dara
places.description: Tony visited this small town in Turkey where they are celebrating a new child. They slaughter a sheep for the meal.

places.name: Breaux Bridge
places.description: Tony went to a backyard boil at a chef's house in this area known for its crawfish. There was also roast corn hash with tasso, venison, stuffed wild wood ducks. The next day, he attended a boucherie, which is a pig slaughter where teams of people quickly work on processing different parts of the pig to make products such as andouille sausage, boudin, ribs, cracklings, and more.

places.name: International Culinary Center
places.description: Originally called the French Culinary Institute (during this taping), Tony visited Dave Arnold to ask what would Jesus eat for the Holiday Special episode.

places.name: Tent City
places.description: Tony visited artists making art in the Tent City, as well as a mass grave that was created after the earthquake. He also met up with actor Sean Penn, who is doing humanitarian work while living in a tent there. They ate chicken pies filled with spicy chicken, picklese, and tomato and deep fried.

places.name: Phnom Penh
places.description: Tony got a haircut on the street where the guy shaved it closer than Tony would have liked.

places.name: Bahia bar de kep cocktail bieres belges les pieds dans l rau
places.description: Tony and Madame Kek took their food they bought at the market to an area along the beach with thatched huts under which they could eat.

places.name: La Chureca
places.description: Home for "churaqueros," this is a trash dump that over 300 families live on (picking through the waste for recyclables). Tony was really disturbed by going there.

places.name: Ybbsitz
places.description: Tony went hunting in the mountains near here. They did not catch anything but his host's family made dinner with venison for him.

places.name: Joh. Springer's Erben
places.description: Tony went shopping for hunting clothes

places.name: Viennese Giant Ferris Wheel
places.description: Oldest ferris wheel in existence.

places.name: West Plains
places.description: Author Daniel Woodrell lives here, one of Tony's favorite authors. His buddy made some squirrel pot pie, venison steaks.

places.name: Ilha do Cumb
places.description: They went here to find acai berries.

places.name: Ilha Mexiana
places.description: Tony and crew took a frightening small plane flight to this island

places.name: WRKO
places.description: Tony did an interview on a right-wing station here.  The Friends of Eddie Coyle is Tony's favorite crime novel, and this impresses the radio host.

places.name: South Boston Candlepin
places.description: Tony played candlepin bowling, where the ball is smaller and the pins are much thinner and harder to knock down.

places.name: Niseko Ski Lodge - Hirafu ニセコスキーロッジ - ひらふ
places.description: Tony skiied while his companions snowboarded down this mountain, It had a been a dream of his to someday ski here

places.name: Green Dirt Farm Creamery
places.description: Local chefs prepared a stuffed lamb for Tony and Zamir. They served it with potato salad with pork jowl and pickled onions, cider vinegar coleslaw, and cornbread.

places.name: Kansas City Chiefs Football
places.description: Tony and Zamir went to a Chiefs tailgate party

places.name: Rovinj
places.description: Tony went on a boat to see mollusk alley, where mussels are harvested the old fashioned way. There are also oysters.

places.name: Motovun
places.description: Tony went with a truffle hunting dog and its owner to find some white truffles in this mountain town. They then cooked with it.

places.name: BIBICh Winery
places.description: Tony tasted wine at this winery that has the same altitude as the Tuscan region. The owner's wife cooked a 12-course meal for them (although it is not a restaurant)

places.name: Konoba Sandra
places.description: This restaurant allowed Tony and his host to cook their fish they caught on the grill. They ate the fish on the boat along with some cheese and bread.

places.name: Doca de Alcântara / Doca do Espanhol
places.description: They launched a boat near here to fish for octopus. Tony seemed miserable and cold with his "inappropriate footwear." The crew members then prepared the freshly caught octopus and they ate it with some cold beer.

places.name: Chinquilho Junqueirense
places.description: Tony played a game called Chinquilho which involves throwing a 7-lb weight at a small explosive

places.name: Sauna Arla
places.description: Everyone told Tony he should do a sauna when he visited Finland. He did it while drinking beer and putting sausage on a grill. He also gets some treatment that causes you to bleed bad toxins out of your back. :/

places.name: Hyvinkää Speedway
places.description: Tony went folk racing here in the snow in a VW Bug

places.name: Popotla
places.description: Seafood cooked on the beach in this small town. Fishermen bring in fresh catch and cook it for you. They had a huge lobster and a crab, Puerto Nuevo style. They chop it lengthwise, fry it and serve with beans & rice.

places.name: Monkey Beach
places.description: Tony went to a tiny remote island called Monkey Beach with lots of macaques. They grilled some fish they bought at a floating seafood market. The island is only accessible by boat.

places.name: Austin
places.description: Tony went to a crab boil at a band's house while in town for SXSW

places.name: Greenhill Farm
places.description: Lamb and beef raised grazing on the land in the farm.

places.name: Sydney Olympic Park
places.description: Tony saw his first Australian rugby game here: The Rabbitohs vs. the Roosters

places.name: Auxerre
places.description: Ludo Lefebvre and Tony went there to visit Ludo's mom in his childhood home.

places.name: Peter Welch's Gym
places.description: Tony visited a boxing gym.

places.name: Shark Dive Xtreme
places.description: Tony scubadived with 350 lb gray nurse sharks.

places.name: Antica Corte Pallavicina Relais
places.description: Culatello is made here, made from pork butt.

places.name: Autodromo Enzo e Dino Ferrari
places.description: Tony drove a Ferrari on this famously difficult  racing track

places.name: Az.Agr. Pedroni Di Pedroni Giuseppe
places.description: Modena Balsamic Vinegar. They had lunch in the attached restaurant: chicken thighs braised in lambrusco; spare ribs slow cooked in white wine with cippolini onions; frittata. All served with droplets of the vinegar

places.name: Gracie Barra Rio Matriz
places.description: Tony watches Ottavio's jiu jitsu match, which she wins!

places.name: Playa Rincon
places.description: Tony took a helicopter to this beach where they had grilled lobster, rice and beans, fried sweet potatoes. For dessert, pina coladas served in a pineapple.

places.name: Zona Colonial
places.description: Tony was invited to a neighborhood birthday party here where he had sancocho, a stew with chicken, beef, yucca, plantains, sliced avocado.

places.name: Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi
places.description: Hotel where he started

places.name: Le Bar de L'Hotel
places.description: Oscar Wilde died here

places.name: The Lodge at Mudbrick
places.description: Tony went boar hunting and then cooked it at the lodge where he was staying.

places.name: Devi Garh
places.description: Tony stayed with his guide at this 18th century fort palace (where the guide worked)

places.name: Kampung Sampireun Resort & Spa
places.description: Resort where everyone gets a lakeside cottage. A man in a boat delivers a breakfast of rice pancakes with brown sugar and coconut milk in the morning.

places.name: Grand Hyatt Singapore
places.description: This is where the crew and Tony are staying but he raved about how great the food is in the hotel, and he normally hates hotel food

places.name: Hazlitt's
places.description: This is the hotel Tony always stayed in. He signed a copy of Kitchen Confidential and put it in his room. It's an author hotel

places.name: Galle Face Hotel
places.description: Tony stayed here. In the open air restaurant, a man scares away the birds from eating guest's breakfasts by shooting them with a slingshot

places.name: The Cliff Preserve
places.description: Tony stayed at this resort in an area nicknamed Patagonia, a mythical land of giants. There is a 5-star restaurant on site that serves ingredients from the property. Steak a la pobre, potatoes, quail eggs, Conger eel from a local fisherman, and a Chilean version of polenta made from corn grown on the premises called chuchoca

places.name: The Historic Murray Hotel
places.description: "I like a hotel with a past," says Tony. He had a drink in the hotel bar with writer Jim Harrison

places.name: The Heathman Hotel
places.description: Tony stayed here, the hotel is rumored to be haunted. Someone jumped off the roof and haunts all the rooms on the way down (all floors with rooms ending in "03")

places.name: Sorgente Su Gologone
places.description: Hotel and restaurant that locals eat at

places.name: Mohonk Mountain House
places.description: "A weirdly wonderful place" built by Quakers in 1870 by Alfred and Albert Smiley (twins). "Strange, but fabulous," Tony says

places.name: Ace Hotel
places.description: A nice but discreet hotel in NYC

places.name: Hôtel Oloffson
places.description: Tony stayed here for a week while visiting Haiti and said it seemed like a spooky fairy tale. He had a rum punch at the hotel bar. He also spoke with the owner of the hotel and they shared some fried shrimp fritters and conch in Creole sauce later in the episode

places.name: Marajó Park Club
places.description: Tony stays here in this remote eco-resort. He goes on a boat and catches a huge pirarucu fish and the hotel staff cooks it for him over a grill with tomatoes, onions, and cilantro.
